data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0107-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — AJC / T12 (Picard representability cone)

- Useful context: run 0010's productive T12 output is already in the working tree and reconciled — `Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean` (real `QuotFunctor`/`Grassmannian` functors), `Picard/GrassmannianRepresentability.lean` (`Grassmannian.representable`, modulo two clean Zariski/sheaf descent leaves), `Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean` (real `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial`), and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`'s `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (Stacks 01XJ, axiom-clean). Durable recipes live in memory `I-0109`, `I-0113`, `I-0120`.

- Open frontier: the `QuotScheme`/representability endgame. `I-0118` flags that the pinned `Scheme.QuotScheme` (only `[IsProper]`/`[LocallyOfFiniteType]`, arbitrary `L`/`E`) is false-as-stated — the Nitsure theorem needs `π` projective, `L` relatively very ample, `E` coherent; Mathlib v4.31 lacks projectivity/ampleness vocabulary. The deepest missing input toward a real Quot statement is `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` (Serre finite-generation of `⊕ₘ Γ(Xₛ, Fₛ⊗Lₛ^{⊗m})`), per `I-0109`.

- Environment note: two T12 Horizon launches this run (`0095-horizon-T12`, `0099-horizon-T12`) both exited 1 at `fable5`/codex harness boot with 0 tokens, producing no diff (see `I-0122`). The accumulated run-0010 AJC work is uncommitted to the project ledger (`.archon-horizon/vcs/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge.git` HEAD is still `6e7f7ae`, run 0005) — a checkpointing lag, not a source problem; the working tree builds as reconciled.

- Adjacent leaves with existing blueprint nodes: `_sectionLinearEquiv` N1–N4 `baseMap`-coherence helpers (`lem:baseMap_*`), and `pullback_tildeIso` (Stacks 01HQ) where the new converse tilde–Γ transport (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_pullback_fromSpec_of_isLocalizedModule`) may help structure the argument.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

Now that most of the necessary architecture is already made, `GrassmannianQuot`, `FlatteningStratification`, `GenericFlatnessGeometric`, etc, you should already commit to the hard sorries and close the representability task.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.
- **Still blocked, do not re-probe**: `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` QC∧GeomIrred conjunct (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8/4.6.1 translate-cover argument, not in Mathlib v4.31); `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` (Quot/Hilbert χ endgame); `finrank_eq_genus`, `Pic0.smooth`, `Pic0.proper` (need tangentSpaceIso resp. Chevalley–Rosenlicht).
- **tangentSpaceIso attack path opened**: the ring-level truncated-exponential splitting is DONE (`Picard/DualNumberUnits.lean`, axiom-clean: `(R[ε])ˣ ≃* Rˣ × (R,+)` + naturality via `mapRingHom`; blueprint `sec:pic0_trunc_exp`, 15 nodes). Supply `hm` by `heq_of_eq (by simp only [defs, Over.homMk_left, Over.map_map_left]; …)` — NEVER `HEq.rfl` (whnf explosion unfolding homOfLE internals). For `hB := rfl` instances, post-process with `rwa [eqToHom_refl, Category.comp_id] at h`.
18	- Proof-irrelevance closes eqToHom-proof mismatches: calc steps `_ = <same with different proof> := rfl` work under the transparency option.
19	- **Opaque-glue trick**: never `set a := 𝒰.glueMorphisms …` — later defeq checks whnf-unfold the multicoequalizer and time out at 2M heartbeats. Instead `obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ : ∃ a, ∀ k, (W k).ι ≫ a = _ := ⟨glueMorphisms …, ι_glueMorphisms …⟩` (a becomes an opaque fvar).
20	- Elements of coproduct presheaves without application-coercion pain: `yonedaEquiv (yoneda.map m ≫ Sigma.ι fam i)`; `yonedaEquiv_comp` is rfl.
21	- Sheaf condition for `zariskiTopology`: `rw [zariskiTopology_eq, Presieve.isSheaf_pretopology]`, get an `OpenCover`, replace by image opens (`Sieve.generate_le_iff` both ways with `IsOpenImmersion.lift`), then `Presieve.isSheafFor_arrows_iff` on an opens family (`opensCover` constructor via `presieve₀_mem_precoverage_iff`).
22	- Chart property: `MorphismProperty.relative.of_exists` needs only ONE explicit pullback per test morphism (`[P.RespectsIso]` handles the rest); pointwise pullbacks of presheaves via bespoke `isPullback_of_app` (evaluationJointlyReflectsLimits + `Types.isPullback_iff`).
23	- `cases`/`obtain` on `Presieve.ofArrows`-membership renames context hypotheses (inaccessible-daggers section variables named `Y`!) — hoist any lemma-application needing section vars BEFORE the case split.

9	
10	**`Grassmannian.representable` was a statement trap and is now fixed + proved modulo two leaves (run 0010 T12 second pass, `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianRepresentability.lean`).**
11	
12	- **Statement fix**: the old QuotFunctorDef skeleton quantified over an ARBITRARY `V : S.Modules` — not the [Nitsure] §1 theorem (which needs `V` locally free of rank `r`, `1 ≤ d ≤ r`) and not known to be true. Also pinned to `Scheme.{0}`: the merged absolute `Grassmannian.scheme/represents` lives at universe 0 (Spec ℤ genuinely terminal), and cross-universe gluing is impossible — any consumer must instantiate `u := 0`.
13	- **Proved axiom-clean**: `representable_of_iso_free` (trivialised case; representing object `(Over.star S).obj (Gr d r)` = `S ⨯ Gr(d,r)` with `prod.fst`) and `representable_restrict` (local representability over a trivialising open, via `restrictIso`). Verified `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.
14	- **UPDATE (third pass): `Scheme.representable_of_openCover` is now PROVED** (see [[zariski-descent-representability-proved]], new file `ZariskiDescentRepresentability.lean` which also hosts `IsZariskiSheafOver`/`overRes*`). **One remaining typed-sorry leaf, TRUE**: `Grassmannian.isZariskiSheaf` (quotient families glue: comparison isos between quotients are UNIQUE when they exist ⟹ cocycle for free; needs module gluing along an open cover — GlueDescent `Scheme.Modules.glue` engine at the cover's glue data). The descent leaf will be reused for Quot and Pic representability. Blueprint nodes: `thm:representability_zariski_descent`, `lem:grassmannian_zariski_sheaf`, `def:zariski_sheaf_over`, `lem:grassmannian_representable_free` (Picard_QuotScheme.tex).
15	
16	**Recipes that landed first-try (reuse):**
17	- **`Over.forgetAdjStar` kills hand-rolled product hom-equivs**: `Hom_{Over S}(T, (Over.star S).obj W) ≃ (T.left ⟶ W)` via `(forgetAdjStar S).homEquiv .symm`; `RepresentableBy.homEquiv_comp` then follows from `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm` + the absolute `homEquiv_comp`. A hand-rolled `Over.homMk (prod.lift ...)` equiv hit an invisible defeq wall in `Over.w`; the adjunction route avoided it entirely.
18	- **Pseudofunctor-coherence squares (tri/comp shuffles)**: state the goal with iso `.hom/.inv` composites; `rw [eq_comm, Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]` into the all-`hom` form; `simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_hom/inv, Iso.app_hom/inv, Category.assoc, Modules.pullbackCongr_hom_app, Modules.pullbackCongr_inv_app, eqToHom_refl, Category.comp_id] at key ⊢` (key = `Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence` instance); finish `exact key.symm` — eqToHom proof args and `(Over.map j).map ψ |>.left ≡ ψ.left` mismatches close by defeq under `respectTransparency false`. Same pattern proved `freeCompare_naturality_core` (via `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr` + `pullbackComp_inv_app_free_map` + `cancel_mono`).
19	- **Eager instance inclusion gotcha**: section `variable [IsLocallyNoetherian S]` is auto-included in every def/theorem mentioning `S` (NOT usage-based, unlike structures) — so `LocallyFreeQuotient` (structure) does NOT carry it but `LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid`/`Grassmannian`/`pullbackAlong` DO. Consequences: `omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in` (placed BEFORE the docstring, after any `set_option … in`) for lemmas that don't need it; any statement mentioning `setoid` at a second base `S'` needs `[IsLocallyNoetherian S']` explicitly.
20	- **Free-sheaf reindex**: `(SheafOfModules.freeFunctor (R := …)).mapIso (Equiv.toIso Equiv.ulift)` bridges `free (ULift (Fin r)) ≅ free (Fin r)` (IsLocallyFreeOfRank trivialises with `ULift` index).

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotFunctorDef
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianQuot
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ZariskiDescentRepresentability
10	
11	/-!
12	# Representability of the relative Grassmannian (`thm:grassmannian_representable`)
13	
14	This file states and attacks the representability of the relative Grassmannian
15	functor `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1` (`Scheme.Grassmannian`,
16	`QuotFunctorDef.lean`) by an `S`-scheme, for `V` a locally free
17	`O_S`-module of rank `r` and `1 ≤ d ≤ r` ([Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2);
18	blueprint `thm:grassmannian_representable`).
19	
20	The statement here **corrects** the earlier skeleton of `QuotFunctorDef.lean`,
21	which quantified over an arbitrary sheaf of modules `V` with no rank data:
22	that form is not the [Nitsure] theorem (whose hypotheses are `V` locally free
23	of rank `r ≥ d ≥ 1`) and is not known to be true for non-quasi-coherent `V`.
24	The universe is pinned to `Scheme.{0}`: the merged absolute chart construction
25	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme` / `.represents`,
26	`GrassmannianQuot.lean`) lives over the genuinely terminal `Spec ℤ` of
27	`Scheme.{0}`, and the relative theorem is obtained from it.
28	
29	## Contents
30	
31	* `Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule` / `Scheme.Grassmannian.congrIso` —
32	  transport of the Grassmannian functor along an isomorphism `V ≅ V'` of the
33	  classified module (pure functoriality, no pseudofunctor coherence).
34	* `Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.toRankQuotient` / `.ofRankQuotient` /
35	  `Scheme.Grassmannian.freeCompare` — for the free module `V = O_S^r`, the
36	  comparison `Grass(O_S^r, d) ≅ (Over.forget S)ᵒᵖ ⋙ Grass(r, d)` with the
37	  merged absolute Grassmannian functor, re-presenting the pulled-back free
38	  module through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso`.  Naturality is the free
39	  coherence `pullbackFreeIso_comp` of `GlueDescent.lean`.
40	* `Scheme.Grassmannian.prodRepresentableBy` — the absolute Grassmannian,
41	  pulled back to `Over S` along the forgetful functor, is representable by
42	  the product `S ⨯ Gr(d, r)` with its first projection (`Spec ℤ` is terminal
43	  in `Scheme.{0}`, so the product is the base change of `Gr(d, r)` to `S`).
44	* `Scheme.Grassmannian.representable_of_iso_free` — representability of
45	  `Grass(V, d)` for globally trivialised `V ≅ O_S^r` (complete proof).
46	* `Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` — the [Nitsure] statement, for `V`
47	  locally free of rank `r`: glue `representable_of_iso_free` over a
48	  trivialising cover of `(S, V)` by Zariski descent of representability
49	  (`Scheme.representable_of_openCover`, proved in
50	  `ZariskiDescentRepresentability.lean`); the remaining leaf is the sheaf
51	  axiom `Grassmannian.isZariskiSheaf`.
52	
53	## References
54	
55	Blueprint: `thm:grassmannian_representable`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`
56	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
57	Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020).
58	-/

70	/-! ## §1. Transport along an isomorphism of the classified module -/
71	
72	namespace LocallyFreeQuotient
73	
74	variable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
75	
76	/-- Transport of a rank-`d` locally free quotient of `V'_T` along an
77	isomorphism `g : V ≅ V'` of the classified modules: precompose the quotient
78	map with the pulled-back isomorphism.  Underlies the invariance
79	`Scheme.Grassmannian.congrIso` of the Grassmannian functor. -/
80	noncomputable def congrModule {V V' : S.Modules} (g : V ≅ V') {d : ℕ} {T : Over S}
81	    (x : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : LocallyFreeQuotient V' d T where
82	  F := x.F
83	  q := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫ x.q
84	  epi :=
85	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
86	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).mapIso g).inv inferInstance
87	      x.q x.epi
88	  locFree := x.locFree
89	
90	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
91	/-- The module-iso transport respects the equivalence of quotients. -/
92	lemma congrModule_rel {V V' : S.Modules} (g : V ≅ V') {d : ℕ} {T : Over S}
93	    {x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
94	    (congrModule g x).Rel (congrModule g y) := by
95	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
96	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
97	  change (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
98	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫ y.q
99	  rw [Category.assoc]
100	  exact congrArg (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫ ·) hf
101	
102	end LocallyFreeQuotient
103	
104	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
105	/-- **Invariance of the Grassmannian functor under isomorphism of the
106	classified module**: an isomorphism `g : V ≅ V'` of sheaves of modules on `S`
107	induces an isomorphism `Grass(V, d) ≅ Grass(V', d)` of the relative
108	Grassmannian functors.  Pure functoriality: the components transport a
109	quotient along `congrModule`, and naturality is the naturality of the
110	pullback pseudofunctor comparisons in the module variable. -/
111	noncomputable def Grassmannian.congrIso {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
112	    {V V' : S.Modules} (g : V ≅ V') (d : ℕ) :
113	    Scheme.Grassmannian V d ≅ Scheme.Grassmannian V' d :=
114	  NatIso.ofComponents
115	    (fun T => Equiv.toIso
116	      { toFun := Quotient.map (LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule g)
117	          (fun _ _ h => LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule_rel g h)
118	        invFun := Quotient.map (LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule g.symm)
119	          (fun _ _ h => LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule_rel g.symm h)
120	        left_inv := fun z => by
121	          induction z using Quotient.ind with
122	          | _ x =>
123	            refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
124	            change ((((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g.symm).inv ≫
125	                (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫ x.q))) ≫ 𝟙 _
126	              = x.q
127	            rw [Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc]
128	            rw [show (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g.symm).inv ≫
129	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g).inv)
130	              = 𝟙 _ by
131	                rw [Functor.mapIso_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.symm_inv,
132	                  ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]]
133	            exact Category.id_comp x.q
134	        right_inv := fun z => by
135	          induction z using Quotient.ind with
136	          | _ x =>
137	            refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
138	            change ((((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫
139	                (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g.symm).inv ≫ x.q))) ≫ 𝟙 _
140	              = x.q
141	            rw [Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc]
142	            rw [show ((((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫
143	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g.symm).inv))
144	              = 𝟙 _ by
145	                rw [Functor.mapIso_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.symm_inv,
146	                  ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]]
147	            exact Category.id_comp x.q })
148	    (fun {T T'} ψ => by
149	      ext z
150	      induction z using Quotient.ind with
151	      | _ x =>
152	        change Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule g
153	            (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ψ.unop x))
154	          = Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ψ.unop
155	            (LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule g x))
156	        refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
157	        change (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T'.unop.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫
158	            (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ.unop) V).inv ≫
159	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q) ≫ 𝟙 _
160	          = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ.unop) V').inv ≫
161	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map
162	              (((Scheme.Modules.pullback T.unop.hom).mapIso g).inv ≫ x.q)
163	        rw [Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
164	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
165	        refine congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q) ?_
166	        -- naturality of the triangle comparison in the module variable at `g.inv`
167	        simpa only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc,
168	          Functor.mapIso_inv, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.comp_map]
169	          using ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.w ψ.unop)).inv ≫
170	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp ψ.unop.left T.unop.hom).inv).naturality g.inv)
171	
172	/-! ## §2. The free case: comparison with the merged absolute Grassmannian
173	
174	For the free module `V = O_S^r` the relative Grassmannian functor agrees with
175	the merged absolute Grassmannian functor `Grass(r, d)`
176	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`) evaluated
177	on the underlying schemes: a quotient of `(T.hom)^* O_S^r` is re-presented as
178	a quotient of `O_T^r` through the free-pullback comparison
179	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso`.  Naturality is the free coherence
180	`pullbackFreeIso_comp` (`GlueDescent.lean`). -/
181	
182	namespace LocallyFreeQuotient
183	
184	variable {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
185	
186	/-- Re-present a rank-`d` locally free quotient of the pulled-back free module
187	`(T.hom)^* O_S^r` as an absolute rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`
188	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient`), through
189	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso`. -/
190	noncomputable def toRankQuotient {r d : ℕ} {T : Over S}
191	    (x : LocallyFreeQuotient
192	      (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) d T) :
193	    AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient r d T.left where
194	  F := x.F
195	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫ x.q
196	  epi :=
197	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
198	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
199	      x.q x.epi
200	  locFree := x.locFree
201	
202	/-- Inverse re-presentation: an absolute rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r` as a
203	quotient of the pulled-back free module `(T.hom)^* O_S^r`. -/
204	noncomputable def ofRankQuotient {r d : ℕ} {T : Over S}
205	    (y : AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient r d T.left) :
206	    LocallyFreeQuotient (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) d T where
207	  F := y.F
208	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom ≫ y.q
209	  epi :=
210	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
211	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom inferInstance
212	      y.q y.epi
213	  locFree := y.locFree
214	
215	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
216	/-- `toRankQuotient` respects the equivalence of quotients. -/
217	lemma toRankQuotient_rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Over S}
218	    {x y : LocallyFreeQuotient
219	      (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) d T}
220	    (h : x.Rel y) : (toRankQuotient x).Rel (toRankQuotient y) := by
221	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
222	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
223	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
224	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫ y.q
225	  rw [Category.assoc]
226	  exact congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫ ·) hf
227	
228	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
229	/-- `ofRankQuotient` respects the equivalence of quotients. -/
230	lemma ofRankQuotient_rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Over S}
231	    {x y : AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient r d T.left}
232	    (h : x.Rel y) : (ofRankQuotient (T := T) x).Rel (ofRankQuotient y) := by
233	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
234	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
235	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
236	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom ≫ y.q
237	  rw [Category.assoc]
238	  exact congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom ≫ ·) hf
239	
240	end LocallyFreeQuotient
241	
242	namespace Grassmannian
243	
244	variable {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
245	
246	/-- The value-level comparison between the relative Grassmannian of the free
247	module and the merged absolute Grassmannian functor. -/
248	noncomputable def freeCompareEquiv (d r : ℕ) (T : Over S) :
249	    Quotient (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid
250	      (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) d T) ≃
251	    Quotient (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid r d T.left) where
252	  toFun := Quotient.map Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.toRankQuotient
253	    (fun _ _ h => Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.toRankQuotient_rel h)
254	  invFun := Quotient.map Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.ofRankQuotient
255	    (fun _ _ h => Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.ofRankQuotient_rel h)
256	  left_inv := fun z => by
257	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
258	    | _ x =>
259	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
260	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom ≫
261	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫ x.q)) ≫ 𝟙 _
262	        = x.q
263	      rw [Category.comp_id, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
264	  right_inv := fun z => by
265	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
266	    | _ x =>
267	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
268	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫
269	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom ≫ x.q)) ≫ 𝟙 _
270	        = x.q
271	      rw [Category.comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
272	
273	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
274	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
275	/-- **Free-pullback coherence for the quotient re-presentation** — the
276	naturality core of `freeCompare`: pulling back along `ψ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over S`
277	commutes with the re-presentation of the source through
278	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso`.  Assembled from the congruence absorptions
279	and the free coherence `pullbackFreeIso_comp` of `GlueDescent.lean`. -/
280	lemma freeCompare_naturality_core {r : ℕ} {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
281	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T'.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫
282	      (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ)
283	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r))).inv
284	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ.left (Fin r)).inv ≫
285	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map
286	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv := by
287	  -- Step A: absorb the `pullbackCongr` cast into the free-pullback comparison.
288	  have hA : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T'.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫
289	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.w ψ)).inv.app
290	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r))
291	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (ψ.left ≫ T.hom) (Fin r)).inv := by
292	    rw [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_inv_app (Over.w ψ)]
293	    rw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_hom_app (Over.w ψ).symm]
294	    exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr (Over.w ψ).symm (Fin r)
295	  -- Step B: invert the free coherence `pullbackFreeIso_comp`.
296	  have hB : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (ψ.left ≫ T.hom) (Fin r)).inv ≫
297	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp ψ.left T.hom).inv.app
298	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r))
299	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ.left (Fin r)).inv ≫
300	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map
301	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).inv := by
302	    rw [← cancel_mono ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map
303	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.hom (Fin r)).hom)]
304	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id,
305	      CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
306	    rw [Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp_inv_app_free_map ψ.left T.hom (Fin r)]
307	    exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
308	  simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv]
309	  rw [← Category.assoc, hA]
310	  exact hB
311	
312	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
313	/-- **Comparison with the absolute Grassmannian** for the free module: the
314	relative Grassmannian functor of `O_S^r` is naturally isomorphic to the merged
315	absolute Grassmannian functor `Grass(r, d)` evaluated on underlying schemes.
316	Components are `freeCompareEquiv`; naturality is
317	`freeCompare_naturality_core`. -/
318	noncomputable def freeCompare (d r : ℕ) :
319	    Scheme.Grassmannian (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) d ≅
320	      (Over.forget S).op ⋙ AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor d r :=
321	  NatIso.ofComponents
322	    (fun T => Equiv.toIso (freeCompareEquiv d r T.unop))
323	    (fun {T T'} ψ => by
324	      ext z
325	      induction z using Quotient.ind with
326	      | _ x =>
327	        change Quotient.mk _ (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.toRankQuotient
328	            (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ψ.unop x))
329	          = Quotient.mk _ (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback ψ.unop.left
330	            (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.toRankQuotient x))
331	        refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
332	        change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T'.unop.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫
333	            (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ.unop)
334	              (SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r))).inv ≫
335	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q) ≫ 𝟙 _
336	          = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ.unop.left (Fin r)).inv ≫
337	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map
338	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso T.unop.hom (Fin r)).inv ≫ x.q)
339	        rw [Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
340	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
341	        exact congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q)
342	          (freeCompare_naturality_core ψ.unop))
343	
344	/-! ## §3. Representability of the pulled-back absolute functor
345	
346	`Scheme.{0}` has binary products (`Spec ℤ` is terminal, pullbacks exist), so
347	the forgetful functor `Over S ⥤ Sch` has the right adjoint `Over.star S`,
348	`Y ↦ (S ⨯ Y, fst)`.  The base change of the absolute Grassmannian to `S` is
349	`(Over.star S).obj Gr(d, r)`, and the adjunction transports the absolute
350	representability (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents`) to `Over S`. -/
351	
352	/-- The merged absolute Grassmannian functor, restricted to `Over S` along the
353	forgetful functor, is representable by the base change
354	`(Over.star S).obj Gr(d, r) = (S ⨯ Gr(d, r), fst)` of the absolute
355	Grassmannian scheme. -/
356	noncomputable def prodRepresentableBy (S : Scheme.{0}) (d r : ℕ)
357	    (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
358	    ((Over.forget S).op ⋙ AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor d r).RepresentableBy
359	      ((Over.star S).obj (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme d r)) where
360	  homEquiv {T} :=
361	    ((Over.forgetAdjStar S).homEquiv T
362	      (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme d r)).symm.trans
363	      (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents d r hd hdr).homEquiv
364	  homEquiv_comp {T T'} f g := by
365	    change (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents d r hd hdr).homEquiv
366	        (((Over.forgetAdjStar S).homEquiv _ _).symm (f ≫ g)) = _
367	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm]
368	    exact (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents d r hd hdr).homEquiv_comp
369	      ((Over.forget S).map f) (((Over.forgetAdjStar S).homEquiv _ _).symm g)
370	
371	/-! ## §4. Representability: trivialised and locally free cases -/
372	
373	/-- **Representability of the relative Grassmannian, trivialised case**: for a
374	globally trivialised module `V ≅ O_S^r` on a locally noetherian `S` and
375	`1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the functor `Grass(V, d)` is representable by the base change
376	`S ⨯ Gr(d, r)` of the absolute Grassmannian.  Complete proof: transport the
377	representability of the pulled-back absolute functor
378	(`prodRepresentableBy`) along the comparisons `congrIso` and `freeCompare`. -/
379	theorem representable_of_iso_free {V : S.Modules} {r : ℕ}
380	    (e : V ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) {d : ℕ}
381	    (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
382	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Scheme.Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) :=
383	  ⟨(Over.star S).obj (AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme d r),
384	    ⟨(prodRepresentableBy S d r hd hdr).ofIso
385	      ((Scheme.Grassmannian.congrIso e d ≪≫ freeCompare d r).symm)⟩⟩
386	
387	end Grassmannian
388	
389	/-! ## §5. Zariski descent of representability (moved)
390	
391	The definitions `overRes` / `overResHom` / `overResLE` / `IsZariskiSheafOver`
392	and the descent theorem `Scheme.representable_of_openCover`
393	(EGA 0_I 4.5.4; Stacks 01JJ) now live in
394	`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/ZariskiDescentRepresentability.lean`, where the
395	theorem is **proved** (via mathlib's
396	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LocalRepresentability`, Stacks 01JJ, applied to the
397	small total functor of `F`). -/
398	
399	/-! ## §6. Restriction of the Grassmannian to an open of the base
400	
401	For a base morphism `j : S' ⟶ S`, restricting the relative Grassmannian
402	functor along `Over.map j : Over S' ⥤ Over S` classifies quotients of the
403	restricted module: the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp` re-presents a
404	quotient of `(T.hom ≫ j)^* V` as a quotient of `T.hom^* (j^* V)`, naturally
405	in `T` (`restrictIso`).  Combined with the trivialised case this yields local
406	representability over each member of a trivialising cover of `(S, V)`
407	(`representable_restrict`), and Zariski descent closes the theorem. -/
408	
409	namespace Grassmannian
410	
411	variable {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
412	
413	/-- Restriction of a quotient family along a base morphism `j : S' ⟶ S`: a
414	family over `(Over.map j).obj T` classifies quotients of `(T.hom ≫ j)^* V`,
415	which the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp` re-presents as a quotient
416	of `T.hom^* (j^* V)`. -/
417	noncomputable def restrictBase {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules}
418	    {d : ℕ} {T : Over S'}
419	    (x : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient V d ((Over.map j).obj T)) :
420	    Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T where
421	  F := x.F
422	  q := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q
423	  epi :=
424	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
425	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom inferInstance
426	      x.q x.epi
427	  locFree := x.locFree
428	
429	/-- Inverse of `restrictBase`: a quotient of `T.hom^* (j^* V)` re-presented
430	as a family over `(Over.map j).obj T`. -/
431	noncomputable def corestrictBase {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules}
432	    {d : ℕ} {T : Over S'}
433	    (y : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T) :
434	    Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient V d ((Over.map j).obj T) where
435	  F := y.F
436	  q := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ y.q
437	  epi :=
438	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
439	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv inferInstance
440	      y.q y.epi
441	  locFree := y.locFree
442	
443	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
444	/-- `restrictBase` respects the equivalence of families. -/
445	lemma restrictBase_rel {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules} {d : ℕ}
446	    {T : Over S'} {x y : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient V d ((Over.map j).obj T)}
447	    (h : x.Rel y) : (restrictBase j x).Rel (restrictBase j y) := by
448	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
449	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
450	  change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
451	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ y.q
452	  rw [Category.assoc]
453	  exact congrArg (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ ·) hf
454	
455	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
456	/-- `corestrictBase` respects the equivalence of families. -/
457	lemma corestrictBase_rel {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules} {d : ℕ}
458	    {T : Over S'}
459	    {x y : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T}
460	    (h : x.Rel y) : (corestrictBase j x).Rel (corestrictBase j y) := by
461	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
462	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
463	  change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
464	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ y.q
465	  rw [Category.assoc]
466	  exact congrArg (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ ·) hf
467	
468	/-- The value-level comparison between the Grassmannian of `V` over the image
469	of `T : Over S'` and the Grassmannian of the restricted module `j^* V`. -/
470	noncomputable def restrictEquiv {S' : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S']
471	    (j : S' ⟶ S) (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) (T : Over S') :
472	    Quotient (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid V d ((Over.map j).obj T)) ≃
473	    Quotient (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid
474	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T) where
475	  toFun := Quotient.map (restrictBase j) (fun _ _ h => restrictBase_rel j h)
476	  invFun := Quotient.map (corestrictBase j) (fun _ _ h => corestrictBase_rel j h)
477	  left_inv := fun z => by
478	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
479	    | _ x =>
480	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
481	      change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫
482	          (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q)) ≫ 𝟙 _
483	        = x.q
484	      rw [Category.comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
485	  right_inv := fun z => by
486	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
487	    | _ x =>
488	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
489	      change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫
490	          (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ x.q)) ≫ 𝟙 _
491	        = x.q
492	      rw [Category.comp_id, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
493	
494	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
495	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
496	/-- **Pseudofunctor coherence for the base restriction** — the naturality
497	core of `restrictIso`: the `pullbackComp` re-presentation commutes with the
498	pullback actions of the two Grassmannian functors.  An instance of
499	`Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence` after normalising the
500	`pullbackCongr` casts to `eqToHom`s. -/
501	lemma restrictBase_naturality_core {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S)
502	    (V : S.Modules) {T T' : Over S'} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
503	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T'.hom j).app V).hom ≫
504	      (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ((Over.map j).map ψ)) V).inv
505	    = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V)).inv ≫
506	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map
507	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom := by
508	  have h₃ : ψ.left ≫ (T.hom ≫ j) = T'.hom ≫ j := by
509	    rw [← Category.assoc, Over.w ψ]
510	  have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence ψ.left T.hom
511	    (Over.w ψ).symm j rfl rfl h₃ V
512	  -- move to the `hom`-form `map compT ≫ tri_ψ'.hom = tri_ψ.hom ≫ compT'`
513	  rw [eq_comm, Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
514	  -- expand the triangle isos and normalise all casts to `eqToHom`
515	  simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.app_hom, Category.assoc,
516	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_hom_app, Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_inv_app,
517	    eqToHom_refl, Category.comp_id] at key ⊢
518	  exact key.symm
519	
520	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
521	/-- **Restriction comparison for the Grassmannian**: for a base morphism
522	`j : S' ⟶ S`, the relative Grassmannian of `V`, restricted along
523	`Over.map j`, is naturally isomorphic to the relative Grassmannian of the
524	restricted module `j^* V` over `S'`. -/
525	noncomputable def restrictIso {S' : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S']
526	    (j : S' ⟶ S) (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
527	    (Over.map j).op ⋙ Scheme.Grassmannian V d ≅
528	      Scheme.Grassmannian ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d :=
529	  NatIso.ofComponents
530	    (fun T => Equiv.toIso (restrictEquiv j V d T.unop))
531	    (fun {T T'} ψ => by
532	      ext z
533	      induction z using Quotient.ind with
534	      | _ x =>
535	        change Quotient.mk _ (restrictBase j
536	            (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ((Over.map j).map ψ.unop) x))
537	          = Quotient.mk _ (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ψ.unop
538	            (restrictBase j x))
539	        refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
540	        change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T'.unop.hom j).app V).hom ≫
541	            (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ((Over.map j).map ψ.unop)) V).inv ≫
542	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q) ≫ 𝟙 _
543	          = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ.unop)
544	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V)).inv ≫
545	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map
546	              (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.unop.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q)
547	        rw [Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
548	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
549	        exact congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q)
550	          (restrictBase_naturality_core j V ψ.unop))
551	
552	/-- **Local representability over a trivialising open**: over an open
553	`U ⊆ S` on which `V` trivialises, the restriction of the Grassmannian
554	functor to `Sch/U` is representable.  Complete proof: compose the
555	restriction comparison `restrictIso` with the trivialised case
556	`representable_of_iso_free` (the trivialisation index is re-normalised from
557	`ULift (Fin r)` to `Fin r` through the free functor). -/
558	theorem representable_restrict {V : S.Modules} {r : ℕ} (U : S.Opens)
559	    (e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj V ≅
560	      SheafOfModules.free (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{0} (Fin r)))
561	    {d : ℕ} (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
562	    ∃ Y : Over U.toScheme,
563	      Nonempty (((Over.map U.ι).op ⋙ Scheme.Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
564	  obtain ⟨Y, ⟨hY⟩⟩ := representable_of_iso_free
565	    (e ≪≫ (SheafOfModules.freeFunctor (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)).mapIso
566	      (Equiv.toIso Equiv.ulift)) hd hdr
567	  exact ⟨Y, ⟨hY.ofIso (restrictIso U.ι V d).symm⟩⟩
568	
569	/-- **The Grassmannian functor is a Zariski sheaf** ([Nitsure] §1; the
570	implicit locality behind the chart construction).  Families of rank-`d`
571	locally free quotients glue along an open cover of the parameter scheme:
572	the target sheaves glue by descent of sheaves of modules along the cover
573	(the `Scheme.Modules.glue` engine of `GlueDescent.lean` at the glue data of
574	the cover), the quotient maps glue by the sheaf property of `Hom`, local
575	freeness and epi-ness are local, and the glued family is unique up to the
576	equivalence `ker q = ker q'` because an isomorphism of quotients commuting
577	with the epimorphisms is unique when it exists, so the local comparison isos
578	agree on overlaps and glue.  [typed `sorry` — the single remaining leaf of
579	`thm:grassmannian_representable`, now that the descent theorem
580	`Scheme.representable_of_openCover` is proved.] -/
581	theorem isZariskiSheaf (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
582	    IsZariskiSheafOver (Scheme.Grassmannian V d) := by
583	  sorry
584	
585	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian** (`thm:grassmannian_representable`,
586	[Nitsure] §1 "Construction of Grassmannian", Exercise (2)): for a locally
587	noetherian `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`, and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`,
588	the functor `Grass(V, d)` is representable by an `S`-scheme.  Proof: `V`
589	trivialises on an open cover of `S`; over each member the functor is
590	representable (`representable_restrict`, via the merged absolute chart
591	construction), and representability descends along the cover
592	(`Scheme.representable_of_openCover`) since the Grassmannian functor is a
593	Zariski sheaf (`isZariskiSheaf`). -/
594	theorem representable {V : S.Modules} {r : ℕ}
595	    (hV : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank V r) {d : ℕ}
596	    (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
597	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Scheme.Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
598	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hU, hloc⟩ := hV
599	  refine representable_of_openCover (Scheme.Grassmannian V d)
600	    (isZariskiSheaf V d) U hU (fun i => ?_)
601	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
602	  exact representable_restrict (U i) e hd hdr
603	
604	end Grassmannian
605	
606	end Scheme
607

64	set_option autoImplicit false
65	
66	universe w v u

68	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
69	
70	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
71	
72	namespace Scheme
73	
74	/-! ## §1. Zariski-local presheaves on `Sch/S` (moved from
75	`GrassmannianRepresentability.lean`) -/
76	
77	/-- The restriction of `T : Over S` to an open `W ⊆ T.left`, as an object of
78	`Over S`. -/
79	noncomputable def overRes {S : Scheme.{0}} (T : Over S) (W : T.left.Opens) :
80	    Over S :=
81	  Over.mk (W.ι ≫ T.hom)
82	
83	/-- The restriction morphism `T|_W ⟶ T` in `Over S`. -/
84	noncomputable def overResHom {S : Scheme.{0}} (T : Over S) (W : T.left.Opens) :
85	    overRes T W ⟶ T :=
86	  Over.homMk W.ι rfl
87	
88	/-- The inclusion `T|_{W'} ⟶ T|_W` in `Over S` attached to `W' ≤ W`. -/
89	noncomputable def overResLE {S : Scheme.{0}} (T : Over S)
90	    {W' W : T.left.Opens} (h : W' ≤ W) : overRes T W' ⟶ overRes T W :=
91	  Over.homMk (T.left.homOfLE h)
92	    ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
93	      (congrArg (· ≫ T.hom) (T.left.homOfLE_ι h)))
94	
95	/-- **The Zariski sheaf axiom for a presheaf on `Sch/S`**: for every
96	`T : Over S` and every open cover `{W k}` of the total space `T.left`, a
97	family of sections of `F` over the restrictions `T|_{W k}` that agree on the
98	pairwise intersections glues to a unique section over `T`.  This is the sheaf
99	condition for the (big) Zariski site of `S`, stated concretely against open
100	covers of the total space; representable presheaves satisfy it, and it is the
101	hypothesis under which local representability descends
102	(`Scheme.representable_of_openCover`). -/
103	def IsZariskiSheafOver {S : Scheme.{0}} (F : (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1) : Prop :=
104	  ∀ (T : Over S) {κ : Type} (W : κ → T.left.Opens), (⨆ k, W k = ⊤) →
105	    ∀ x : ∀ k, F.obj (Opposite.op (overRes T (W k))),
106	    (∀ k l : κ, F.map (overResLE T (inf_le_left : W k ⊓ W l ≤ W k)).op (x k)
107	      = F.map (overResLE T (inf_le_right : W k ⊓ W l ≤ W l)).op (x l)) →
108	    ∃! x₀ : F.obj (Opposite.op T), ∀ k, F.map (overResHom T (W k)).op x₀ = x k
109	
110	/-! ## §2. Generic helpers -/
111	
112	/-- A commutative square of `Type`-valued presheaves that is a pointwise
113	pullback is a pullback square (evaluation functors jointly reflect limits). -/
114	lemma isPullback_of_app {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
115	    {P X Y Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w} {fst : P ⟶ X} {snd : P ⟶ Y} {f : X ⟶ Z} {g : Y ⟶ Z}
116	    (h : ∀ c : Cᵒᵖ, IsPullback (fst.app c) (snd.app c) (f.app c) (g.app c)) :
117	    IsPullback fst snd f g := by
118	  have w : fst ≫ f = snd ≫ g := by
119	    apply NatTrans.ext; funext c
120	    rw [NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.comp_app]
121	    exact (h c).w
122	  exact
123	    { w := w
124	      isLimit' := ⟨evaluationJointlyReflectsLimits _ fun c => by
125	        refine IsLimit.equivOfNatIsoOfIso
126	          (cospanCompIso ((CategoryTheory.evaluation Cᵒᵖ (Type w)).obj c) f g).symm
127	          _ _ ?_ ((h c).isLimit)
128	        refine Cone.ext (Iso.refl _) ?_
129	        rintro (_ | (_ | _)) <;> cat_disch⟩ }
130	
131	/-- The open cover of a scheme attached to a family of opens with `⨆ = ⊤`. -/
132	@[simps I₀ X f]
133	noncomputable def opensCover (T : Scheme.{0}) {κ : Type} (W : κ → T.Opens)
134	    (hW : ⨆ k, W k = ⊤) : T.OpenCover where
135	  I₀ := κ
136	  X k := (W k).toScheme
137	  f k := (W k).ι
138	  mem₀ := by
139	    rw [presieve₀_mem_precoverage_iff]

663	/-! ## §4. The relative Grassmannian functor -/
664	
665	section Grassmannian
666	
667	variable [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
668	
669	/-- A **rank-`d` locally free quotient of `V_T`** over `T : Over S`
670	(`def:grassmannian_scheme`, [Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2)): a sheaf of modules
671	`F` on `T`, locally free of rank `d`, with an epimorphism from the pullback
672	`V_T` of `V` along the structure morphism. -/
673	structure LocallyFreeQuotient (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) (T : Over S) : Type (u + 1) where
674	  /-- The quotient sheaf on `T`. -/
675	  F : (T.left : Scheme.{u}).Modules
676	  /-- The quotient map out of the pulled-back bundle `V_T`. -/
677	  q : (Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).obj V ⟶ F
678	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism. -/
679	  epi : Epi q
680	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
681	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
682	
683	namespace LocallyFreeQuotient
684	
685	variable {V : S.Modules} {d : ℕ}
686	
687	/-- Equivalence of rank-`d` quotients: an isomorphism of the targets
688	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, `ker q = ker q'`). -/
689	def Rel {T : Over S} (x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : Prop :=
690	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
691	
692	lemma rel_refl {T : Over S} (x : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : x.Rel x :=
693	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
694	
695	lemma rel_symm {T : Over S} {x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
696	    y.Rel x := by
697	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
698	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
699	
700	lemma rel_trans {T : Over S} {x y z : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T}
701	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
702	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
703	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
704	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
705	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
706	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
707	
708	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid. -/
709	instance setoid (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) (T : Over S) :
710	    Setoid (LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) where
711	  r := Rel
712	  iseqv := ⟨rel_refl, rel_symm, rel_trans⟩
713	
714	/-- The pullback action on a rank-`d` quotient along `ψ : T' ⟶ T` of
715	`Over S`: pull the sheaf and the quotient map back along `ψ.left`, matching
716	the `V`-side through `pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ)`. -/
717	noncomputable def pullbackAlong {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
718	    (x : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T' where
719	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).obj x.F
720	  q := (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
721	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map x.q
722	  epi :=
723	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
724	      (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv inferInstance
725	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map x.q)
726	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _
727	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left) inferInstance _ _ x.q x.epi)
728	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ.left x.locFree
729	
730	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation. -/
731	lemma pullbackAlong_rel {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
732	    {x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
733	    (pullbackAlong ψ x).Rel (pullbackAlong ψ y) := by
734	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
735	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).mapIso f, ?_⟩
736	  change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
737	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map x.q) ≫
738	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map f.hom
739	    = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
740	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map y.q
741	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map_comp]
742	  exact congrArg
743	    (fun m => (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
744	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map m) hf
745	
746	end LocallyFreeQuotient

750	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of
751	rank-`d` locally free quotients of a module `V` on a noetherian base `S`
752	(`def:grassmannian_scheme`, [Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2)): an `S`-scheme
753	`T → S` is sent to the set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of rank-`d`
754	locally free quotients `q : V_T ↠ F` (`Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient`), and a
755	morphism to the pullback of quotients.  In the locally noetherian regime
756	this agrees with the Quot-functor special case
757	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` of the blueprint (a flat finitely
758	presented module with constant fibre dimension `d` is locally free of rank
759	`d`); the locally-free encoding is the form valid over an arbitrary base and
760	the one matched by the merged chart construction
761	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`). -/
762	noncomputable def Grassmannian (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
763	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u + 1) where
764	  obj T := Quotient (LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid V d T.unop)

767	  map_id T := by
768	    ext z

770	    | _ x =>

772	        = Quotient.mk _ x
773	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
774	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app x.F, ?_⟩
775	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w (𝟙 T.unop)) V).inv ≫
776	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Over.Hom.left (𝟙 T.unop))).map x.q) ≫
777	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
778	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.app x.F
779	        = x.q
780	      rw [Category.assoc,
781	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc x.q,
782	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.naturality x.q]
783	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence (Over.id_left T.unop)
784	        (Over.w (𝟙 T.unop)) V
785	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc]
786	      rw [reassoc_of% key]

789	    ext z
790	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
791	    | _ x =>
792	      change Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong (h.unop ≫ g.unop) x)
793	        = Quotient.mk _
794	          (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong h.unop (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong g.unop x))
795	      refine Quotient.sound
796	        ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
797	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩

156	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
157	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
158	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
159	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
160	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
161	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
162	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
163	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
164	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
165	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
166	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
167	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
168	  equalizer a b

170	/-- **Lift into the glued sheaf** (`def:gr_modules_glueHom`-adjacent primitive): a family of
171	morphisms `k i : W ⟶ (ι_i)_* M_i` whose two overlap restrictions agree (the hypothesis
172	`hk`, stated against the two legs of the descent equalizer) lifts to a morphism
173	`W ⟶ glue D M g _ _`. This is `equalizer.lift` for the descent equalizer of pushforwards;
174	it is the vehicle by which the tautological quotient is assembled from the chart
175	quotients. Project-local. -/
176	noncomputable def glueLift (D : Scheme.GlueData)
177	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
178	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
179	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
180	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
181	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
182	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
183	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
184	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
185	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
186	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
187	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
188	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
189	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
190	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
191	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))
192	    {W : D.glued.Modules}
193	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
194	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
195	      k p.1 ≫
196	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
197	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
198	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
199	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
200	        = k p.2 ≫
201	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
202	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
203	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
204	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
205	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
206	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
207	            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
208	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
209	            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
210	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
211	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) :
212	    W ⟶ glue D M g _hC1 _hC2 :=
213	  equalizer.lift (Pi.lift k) (by
214	    apply Pi.hom_ext
215	    intro p
216	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.Pi.lift_π_assoc]
217	    exact hk p)
218	
219	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
220	
221	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
222	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
223	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
224	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
225	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
226	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
227	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
228	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
229	morphisms. -/
230

40	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
41	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
42	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
43	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
44	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
45	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
46	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
47	
48	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
49	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
50	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
51	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
52	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
53
