55	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
56	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
57	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
58	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
59	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
60	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
61	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
62	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
63	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
64	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
65	
66	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
67	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
68	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
69	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/

71	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
72	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
73	
74	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg

76	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
77	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
78	for the module cocycle (C2). -/

80	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
81	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
82	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
83	    Category.assoc]
84

86	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target

88	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
89	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for

96	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]

98	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]

219	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
220	
221	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
222	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
223	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
224	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
225	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
226	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
227	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
228	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
229	morphisms. -/
230	
231	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
232	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
233	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
234	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
235	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
236	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
237	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
238	  constructor
239	  intro V
240	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
241	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
242	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
243	  apply zigzag_isConnected
244	  intro s t
245	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
246	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
247	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
248	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
249	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
250	
251	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
252	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
253	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
254	Project-local. -/
255	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
256	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
257	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
258	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
259	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
260	
261	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
262	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
263	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
264	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
265	    eqToHom (congrArg
266	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
267	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
268	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
269	  subst h; simp
270	
271	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
272	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
273	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
274	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
275	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
276	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
277	    (I : Type u) :
278	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
279	      = eqToIso (congrArg
280	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
281	          h) := by
282	  subst h; simp
283	
284	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
285	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
286	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
287	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
288	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
289	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
290	Project-local. -/
291	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
292	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
293	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
294	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
295	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
296	  intro i
297	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
298	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
299	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
300	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
301	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
302	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
303	
304	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
305	
306	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

308	/-- **Unit coherence (`map_id` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`).** The
309	Mathlib free-pullback comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` at the identity
310	morphism agrees, on the unit sheaf, with the scheme-level pseudofunctor identity
311	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackId`. Project-local: bridges `pullbackObjFreeIso` to the
312	pseudofunctor `pullbackId`. -/
313	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id {T : Scheme.{u}} :
314	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T))
315	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) := by
316	  rw [← SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit,
317	    Equiv.symm_apply_eq, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
318	  have h := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv Adjunction.id
319	    (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)))
320	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
321	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at h
322	  rw [← h]
323	  -- term-mode `id_comp` (positional `rw [Category.id_comp]` hits the `T.Modules` instance diamond)
324	  refine Eq.trans ?_ (Category.id_comp _).symm
325	  -- the `conjugateEquiv` term sits in unfolded `SheafOfModules` form; bridge to the
326	  -- scheme-level pseudofunctor coherence by defeq, then it equals `(pushforwardId).inv`.
327	  have key : (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv Adjunction.id
328	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)))
329	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom)
330	      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId T).inv :=
331	    Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom T
332	  rw [key]
333	  ext Y
334	  -- both sides evaluate the unit section `1` through identity-like maps
335	  rfl
336	
337	/-- **Free coherence (`map_id`).** `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) I` agrees, on the free sheaf,
338	with the pseudofunctor identity `pullbackId`. Reduces to `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id` by
339	coproduct extensionality (`free = ∐ unit`). Project-local. -/
340	lemma pullbackFreeIso_id {T : Scheme.{u}} (I : Type u) :
341	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) I).hom
342	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I) := by
343	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final (𝟙 T)
344	  -- Use the `SheafOfModules.pullback` form in the cofan: the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` wrapper

349	    (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) _ _ (fun i => ?_)

357	        (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)) i).trans
358	      ((congrArg (· ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := T.ringCatSheaf) i) pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id).trans
359	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.naturality
360	          (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := T.ringCatSheaf) i)).symm)

362	/-- **Mate compatibility of `homEquiv`.** For adjunctions `adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁`, `adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂`

input.prompt:
You are exploring a Lean 4 workspace. Read parts of /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean (~5700 lines).

TASK: Report on three patterns used in this file:

1. **How the C1/C2 cocycle hypotheses of `Scheme.Modules.glue` were discharged** for the Grassmannian glue data (`theGlueData d r`). Find where `Scheme.Modules.glue` is invoked (grep for "Modules.glue"), and quote the terms/lemmas supplied for the `_hC1` and `_hC2` arguments (e.g. lemma names like `transition_C1`, `transition_cocycle` etc.), with their full statements. If C2 was proved by an explicit computation rather than a uniqueness trick, say so and summarize the proof technique in 5-10 lines.

2. **How local-freeness (`SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ... d`) of the glued/tautological sheaf was proved** from the chart sheaves being free. Find the declaration (grep for "IsLocallyFreeOfRank" in the file), quote its statement and sketch its proof technique (which lemmas it uses to produce the trivializing cover on the glued scheme — e.g. how opens of a chart are transported to opens of the glued scheme, what iso is used for the restriction). Quote the key intermediate lemma names and statements.

3. **The `Abelian.epiDesc` uniqueness pattern** around lines 4929-5100: quote in full (a) `pullback_map_cover_faithful` (line ~4934) — full statement; (b) the surrounding proof that uses `Abelian.epiDesc` to compare two quotients that agree on a cover — quote enough of that proof (~lines 5029-5100) to see the exact incantations: how `Epi` instances are provided, how `Abelian.epiDesc`+`comp_epiDesc` are invoked, how the two descents are shown mutually inverse. Also report whether `SheafOfModules` abelian-ness is used as an instance directly (is there `Abelian (SheafOfModules ...)` available, imported from where?).

4. Also grep the file for "epi" case-insensitively and list any general-purpose lemmas about epimorphisms of sheaves of modules being detected on a cover (statement + line).

Return a structured report with exact quotes and line numbers.

3348	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i) ≫
3349	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).counit.app (M i) :=
3350	    Adjunction.homEquiv_counit _ _ _ _
3351	  refine ⟨glueRestrictionInv D M g hC1 hC2 i, ?_, ?_⟩
3352	  · -- `r_i ≫ σ_i = 𝟙`: joint detection by the restricted projections; the `j`-th
3353	    -- component is the transposed pair-`(i,j)` equalizer condition (obligation 3)
3354	    apply glueRestrict_hom_ext D M g hC1 hC2
3355	    intro j
3356	    rw [Category.assoc, glueRestrictionInv_pullback_map_glueProj,
3357	      glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent]
3358	    exact (Category.id_comp _).symm
3359	  · -- `σ_i ≫ r_i = 𝟙`: the self-component collapses by (C1) + the counit triangle
3360	    -- (obligation 2)

3362	    exact (whisker_eq _ hr).trans ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
3363	      ((eq_whisker (glueRestrictionInv_pullback_map_glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i i) _).trans
3364	        (glueChartComponent_self_counit D M g hC1 i)))

3366	/-- **The restriction isomorphism of the glued sheaf**
3367	(`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`): the canonical identification
3368	`ι_i^* (glue D M g …) ≅ M i` of the chart restriction of the glued sheaf with the
3369	`i`-th input sheaf, with underlying morphism the adjoint transpose of the `i`-th
3370	descent-equalizer projection. Project-local. -/
3371	noncomputable def glueRestrictionIso (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
3372	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
3373	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
3374	    (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
3375	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
3376	    (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
3377	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
3378	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
3379	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
3380	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
3381	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
3382	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
3383	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
3384	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
3385	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) (i : D.J) :
3386	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ≅ M i :=
3387	  haveI := isIso_glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i
3388	  asIso (glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i)

1	/-

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Restrict
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.LocallyDirected
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Local
11	public import Mathlib.Geometry.RingedSpace.PresheafedSpace.Gluing
12	
13	/-!
14	# Gluing Schemes
15	
16	Given a family of gluing data of schemes, we may glue them together.
17	Also see the section about "locally directed" gluing,
18	which is a special case where the conditions are easier to check.
19	
20	## Main definitions
21	
22	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`: A structure containing the family of gluing data.
23	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.glued`: The glued scheme.
24	    This is defined as the multicoequalizer of `∐ V i j ⇉ ∐ U i`, so that the general colimit API
25	    can be used.
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.ι`: The immersion `ι i : U i ⟶ glued` for each `i : J`.
27	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.isoCarrier`: The isomorphism between the underlying space
28	  of the glued scheme and the gluing of the underlying topological spaces.
29	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.OpenCover.gluedCover`: The glue data associated with an open cover.
30	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.OpenCover.fromGlued`: The canonical morphism
31	  `𝒰.gluedCover.glued ⟶ X`. This has an `is_iso` instance.
32	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphisms`: We may glue a family of compatible
33	  morphisms defined on an open cover of a scheme.
34	
35	## Main results
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.ι_isOpenImmersion`: The map `ι i : U i ⟶ glued`
38	  is an open immersion for each `i : J`.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.ι_jointly_surjective` : The underlying maps of
40	  `ι i : U i ⟶ glued` are jointly surjective.
41	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.vPullbackConeIsLimit` : `V i j` is the pullback
42	  (intersection) of `U i` and `U j` over the glued space.
43	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.ι_eq_iff` : `ι i x = ι j y` if and only if they coincide
44	  when restricted to `V i i`.
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData.isOpen_iff` : A subset of the glued scheme is open iff
46	  all its preimages in `U i` are open.
47	
48	## Implementation details
49	
50	All the hard work is done in `Mathlib/Geometry/RingedSpace/PresheafedSpace/Gluing.lean` where we
51	glue presheafed spaces, sheafed spaces, and locally ringed spaces.
52	
53	-/
54	
55	@[expose] public section
56	
57	
58	noncomputable section
59	
60	universe v u
61	
62	open TopologicalSpace CategoryTheory Opposite Topology
63	
64	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicGeometry.PresheafedSpace
65	
66	open CategoryTheory.GlueData
67	
68	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
69	
70	namespace Scheme
71	
72	/-- A family of gluing data consists of
73	1. An index type `J`
74	2. A scheme `U i` for each `i : J`.
75	3. A scheme `V i j` for each `i j : J`.
76	  (Note that this is `J × J → Scheme` rather than `J → J → Scheme` to connect to the
77	  limits library easier.)
78	4. An open immersion `f i j : V i j ⟶ U i` for each `i j : ι`.
79	5. A transition map `t i j : V i j ⟶ V j i` for each `i j : ι`.
80	
81	such that
82	6. `f i i` is an isomorphism.
83	7. `t i i` is the identity.
84	8. `V i j ×[U i] V i k ⟶ V i j ⟶ V j i` factors through `V j k ×[U j] V j i ⟶ V j i` via some
85	    `t' : V i j ×[U i] V i k ⟶ V j k ×[U j] V j i`.
86	9. `t' i j k ≫ t' j k i ≫ t' k i j = 𝟙 _`.
87	
88	We can then glue the schemes `U i` together by identifying `V i j` with `V j i`, such
89	that the `U i`'s are open subschemes of the glued space.
90	-/
91	structure GlueData extends CategoryTheory.GlueData Scheme where
92	  f_open : ∀ i j, IsOpenImmersion (f i j)
93	
94	attribute [instance] GlueData.f_open

96	namespace GlueData

98	variable (D : GlueData.{u})
99	
100	local notation "𝖣" => D.toGlueData
101

103	abbrev toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData : LocallyRingedSpace.GlueData :=
104	  { f_open := D.f_open
105	    toGlueData := 𝖣.mapGlueData forgetToLocallyRingedSpace }

108	    LocallyRingedSpace.IsOpenImmersion ((D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData).toGlueData.f i j) := by
109	  apply GlueData.f_open

111	instance (i j : 𝖣.J) :
112	    SheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion
113	      (D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData.toSheafedSpaceGlueData.toGlueData.f i j) := by
114	  apply GlueData.f_open
115	
116	instance (i j : 𝖣.J) :
117	    PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion
118	      (D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData.toSheafedSpaceGlueData.toPresheafedSpaceGlueData.toGlueData.f

129	def gluedScheme : Scheme := by

132	  intro x

134	  obtain ⟨j, z, hz⟩ := (D.U i).affineCover.exists_eq y

136	    D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData.toGlueData.ι i, ?_⟩
137	  constructor
138	  · simp only [LocallyRingedSpace.comp_toHom, PresheafedSpace.comp_base,
139	      TopCat.hom_comp, ContinuousMap.coe_comp, Set.range_comp]
140	    exact Set.mem_image_of_mem _ ⟨z, hz⟩
141	  · infer_instance

145	    (HasColimit.isoOfNatIso (𝖣.diagramIso forgetToLocallyRingedSpace).symm)
146

149	      LocallyRingedSpace.forgetToSheafedSpace ⋙ SheafedSpace.forget CommRingCat))
150

152	  inferInstanceAs (PreservesColimit (𝖣.diagram).multispan (forgetToTop ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget _))

154	instance : HasMulticoequalizer 𝖣.diagram :=
155	  hasColimit_of_created _ forgetToLocallyRingedSpace

157	/-- The glued scheme of a glued space. -/
158	abbrev glued : Scheme :=
159	  𝖣.glued

163	  𝖣.ι i

165	/-- The gluing as sheafed spaces is isomorphic to the gluing as presheafed spaces. -/

167	    D.glued.toLocallyRingedSpace ≅ D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData.toGlueData.glued :=
168	  𝖣.gluedIso forgetToLocallyRingedSpace

171	    D.toLocallyRingedSpaceGlueData.toGlueData.ι i ≫
172	      D.isoLocallyRingedSpace.inv = (𝖣.ι i).toLRSHom :=
173	  𝖣.ι_gluedIso_inv forgetToLocallyRingedSpace i
174	
175	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
176	instance ι_isOpenImmersion (i : D.J) : IsOpenImmersion (𝖣.ι i) := by
177	  rw [IsOpenImmersion, ← D.ι_isoLocallyRingedSpace_inv]; infer_instance
178	
179	theorem ι_jointly_surjective (x : 𝖣.glued.carrier) :
180	    ∃ (i : D.J) (y : (D.U i).carrier), D.ι i y = x :=
181	  𝖣.ι_jointly_surjective forget x

184	@[simp (high), reassoc]
185	theorem glue_condition (i j : D.J) : D.t i j ≫ D.f j i ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i :=
186	  𝖣.glue_condition i j

188	/-- The pullback cone spanned by `V i j ⟶ U i` and `V i j ⟶ U j`.
189	This is a pullback diagram (`vPullbackConeIsLimit`). -/
190	def vPullbackCone (i j : D.J) : PullbackCone (D.ι i) (D.ι j) :=
191	  PullbackCone.mk (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (by simp)
192	
193	/-- The following diagram is a pullback, i.e. `Vᵢⱼ` is the intersection of `Uᵢ` and `Uⱼ` in `X`.
194	```
195	Vᵢⱼ ⟶ Uᵢ
196	 |      |
197	 ↓      ↓
198	 Uⱼ ⟶ X
199	```
200	-/

202	  𝖣.vPullbackConeIsLimitOfMap forgetToLocallyRingedSpace i j

217	    LocallyRingedSpace.GlueData.isoSheafedSpace _

274	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒰 : OpenCover.{u} X)
275	
276	/-- (Implementation) the transition maps in the glue data associated with an open cover. -/
277	def gluedCoverT' (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
278	    pullback (pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y)) (pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f z)) ⟶
279	      pullback (pullback.fst (𝒰.f y) (𝒰.f z)) (pullback.fst (𝒰.f y) (𝒰.f x)) := by
280	  refine (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _).hom ≫ ?_
281	  refine ?_ ≫ (pullbackSymmetry _ _).hom
282	  refine ?_ ≫ (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _).inv
283	  refine pullback.map _ _ _ _ (pullbackSymmetry _ _).hom (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) ?_ ?_
284	  · simp [pullback.condition]
285	  · simp
286	
287	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
288	@[simp, reassoc]
289	theorem gluedCoverT'_fst_fst (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
290	    𝒰.gluedCoverT' x y z ≫ pullback.fst _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _ =
291	      pullback.fst _ _ ≫ pullback.snd _ _ := by
292	  delta gluedCoverT'; simp
293	
294	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
295	@[simp, reassoc]
296	theorem gluedCoverT'_fst_snd (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
297	    gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z ≫ pullback.fst _ _ ≫ pullback.snd _ _ =
298	      pullback.snd _ _ ≫ pullback.snd _ _ := by
299	  delta gluedCoverT'; simp
300	
301	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
302	@[simp, reassoc]
303	theorem gluedCoverT'_snd_fst (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
304	    gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z ≫ pullback.snd _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _ =
305	      pullback.fst _ _ ≫ pullback.snd _ _ := by
306	  delta gluedCoverT'; simp
307	
308	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
309	@[simp, reassoc]
310	theorem gluedCoverT'_snd_snd (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
311	    gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z ≫ pullback.snd _ _ ≫ pullback.snd _ _ =
312	      pullback.fst _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _ := by
313	  delta gluedCoverT'; simp
314	
315	theorem glued_cover_cocycle_fst (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
316	    gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z ≫ gluedCoverT' 𝒰 y z x ≫ gluedCoverT' 𝒰 z x y ≫ pullback.fst _ _ =
317	      pullback.fst _ _ := by
318	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;> simp
319	
320	theorem glued_cover_cocycle_snd (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
321	    gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z ≫ gluedCoverT' 𝒰 y z x ≫ gluedCoverT' 𝒰 z x y ≫ pullback.snd _ _ =
322	      pullback.snd _ _ := by
323	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;> simp [pullback.condition]
324	
325	theorem glued_cover_cocycle (x y z : 𝒰.I₀) :
326	    gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z ≫ gluedCoverT' 𝒰 y z x ≫ gluedCoverT' 𝒰 z x y = 𝟙 _ := by
327	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;> simp_rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc]
328	  · apply glued_cover_cocycle_fst
329	  · apply glued_cover_cocycle_snd

331	/-- The glue data associated with an open cover.
332	The canonical isomorphism `𝒰.gluedCover.glued ⟶ X` is provided by `𝒰.fromGlued`. -/
333	@[simps]
334	def gluedCover : Scheme.GlueData.{u} where
335	  J := 𝒰.I₀
336	  U := 𝒰.X
337	  V := fun ⟨x, y⟩ => pullback (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y)
338	  f _ _ := pullback.fst _ _
339	  f_id _ := inferInstance
340	  t _ _ := (pullbackSymmetry _ _).hom
341	  t_id x := by simp
342	  t' x y z := gluedCoverT' 𝒰 x y z
343	  t_fac x y z := by apply pullback.hom_ext <;> simp
344	  -- The `cocycle` field could have been `by tidy` but lean timeouts.
345	  cocycle x y z := glued_cover_cocycle 𝒰 x y z
346	  f_open _ := inferInstance
347	
348	/-- The canonical morphism from the gluing of an open cover of `X` into `X`.
349	This is an isomorphism, as witnessed by an `IsIso` instance. -/
350	def fromGlued : 𝒰.gluedCover.glued ⟶ X := by
351	  fapply Multicoequalizer.desc
352	  · exact fun x => 𝒰.f x
353	  rintro ⟨x, y⟩
354	  change pullback.fst _ _ ≫ _ = ((pullbackSymmetry _ _).hom ≫ pullback.fst _ _) ≫ _
355	  simpa using! pullback.condition
356	
357	@[simp, reassoc]
358	theorem ι_fromGlued (x : 𝒰.I₀) : 𝒰.gluedCover.ι x ≫ 𝒰.fromGlued = 𝒰.f x :=
359	  Multicoequalizer.π_desc _ _ _ _ _
360	
361	theorem fromGlued_injective : Function.Injective 𝒰.fromGlued := by
362	  intro x y h
363	  obtain ⟨i, x, rfl⟩ := 𝒰.gluedCover.ι_jointly_surjective x
364	  obtain ⟨j, y, rfl⟩ := 𝒰.gluedCover.ι_jointly_surjective y
365	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply] at h
366	  simp_rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_base] at h
367	  rw [ι_fromGlued, ι_fromGlued] at h
368	  let e :=
369	    (TopCat.pullbackConeIsLimit _ _).conePointUniqueUpToIso
370	      (isLimitOfHasPullbackOfPreservesLimit Scheme.forgetToTop (𝒰.f i) (𝒰.f j))
371	  rw [𝒰.gluedCover.ι_eq_iff]
372	  use e.hom ⟨⟨x, y⟩, h⟩
373	  constructor
374	  · erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply e.hom,
375	      IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ WalkingCospan.left]
376	    rfl
377	  · erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply e.hom, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst,
378	      IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ WalkingCospan.right]
379	    rfl
380	
381	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
382	instance (x : 𝒰.gluedCover.glued.carrier) :
383	    IsIso (𝒰.fromGlued.stalkMap x) := by
384	  obtain ⟨i, x, rfl⟩ := 𝒰.gluedCover.ι_jointly_surjective x
385	  have := Hom.stalkMap_congr_hom _ _ (𝒰.ι_fromGlued i) x
386	  rw [Hom.stalkMap_comp, ← IsIso.eq_comp_inv] at this
387	  rw [this]
388	  infer_instance
389	
390	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
391	theorem isOpenMap_fromGlued : IsOpenMap 𝒰.fromGlued := by
392	  intro U hU
393	  rw [isOpen_iff_forall_mem_open]
394	  intro x hx
395	  rw [𝒰.gluedCover.isOpen_iff] at hU
396	  use 𝒰.fromGlued '' U ∩ Set.range (𝒰.f (𝒰.idx x))
397	  use Set.inter_subset_left
398	  constructor
399	  · rw [← Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range]
400	    apply (𝒰.f (𝒰.idx x)).isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap
401	    convert! hU (𝒰.idx x) using 1
402	    simp only [← ι_fromGlued, gluedCover_U, Hom.comp_base, TopCat.hom_comp, ContinuousMap.coe_comp,
403	      Set.preimage_comp]
404	    congr! 1
405	    exact Set.preimage_image_eq _ 𝒰.fromGlued_injective
406	  · exact ⟨hx, 𝒰.covers x⟩
407	
408	theorem isOpenEmbedding_fromGlued : IsOpenEmbedding 𝒰.fromGlued :=
409	  .of_continuous_injective_isOpenMap (by fun_prop) 𝒰.fromGlued_injective 𝒰.isOpenMap_fromGlued
410	
411	instance : Epi 𝒰.fromGlued.base := by
412	  rw [TopCat.epi_iff_surjective]
413	  intro x
414	  obtain ⟨y, h⟩ := 𝒰.covers x
415	  use 𝒰.gluedCover.ι (𝒰.idx x) y
416	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
417	  rw [← 𝒰.ι_fromGlued (𝒰.idx x)] at h
418	  exact h
419	
420	instance : IsOpenImmersion 𝒰.fromGlued :=
421	  IsOpenImmersion.of_isIso_stalkMap _ 𝒰.isOpenEmbedding_fromGlued
422	
423	instance : IsIso 𝒰.fromGlued :=
424	  let F := Scheme.forgetToLocallyRingedSpace ⋙ LocallyRingedSpace.forgetToSheafedSpace ⋙
425	    SheafedSpace.forgetToPresheafedSpace
426	  have : IsIso (F.map (fromGlued 𝒰)) := by
427	    change IsIso 𝒰.fromGlued.toPshHom
428	    apply PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.to_iso
429	  isIso_of_reflects_iso _ F

431	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
432	/-- Given an open cover of `X`, and a morphism `𝒰.X x ⟶ Y` for each open subscheme in the cover,
433	such that these morphisms are compatible in the intersection (pullback), we may glue the morphisms
434	together into a morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
435	
436	Note:
437	If `X` is exactly (defeq to) the gluing of `U i`, then using `Multicoequalizer.desc` suffices.
438	-/
439	def glueMorphisms (𝒰 : OpenCover.{v} X) {Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : ∀ x, 𝒰.X x ⟶ Y)
440	    (hf : ∀ x y, pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y) ≫ f x = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ f y) :
441	    X ⟶ Y := by
442	  refine inv 𝒰.ulift.fromGlued ≫ ?_
443	  fapply Multicoequalizer.desc
444	  · exact fun i ↦ f _
445	  rintro ⟨i, j⟩
446	  dsimp
447	  change pullback.fst _ _ ≫ f _ = (_ ≫ _) ≫ f _
448	  simpa [pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst] using hf _ _
449	
450	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
451	theorem hom_ext (𝒰 : OpenCover.{v} X) {Y : Scheme} (f₁ f₂ : X ⟶ Y)
452	    (h : ∀ x, 𝒰.f x ≫ f₁ = 𝒰.f x ≫ f₂) : f₁ = f₂ := by
453	  rw [← cancel_epi 𝒰.ulift.fromGlued]
454	  apply Multicoequalizer.hom_ext
455	  intro x
456	  rw [fromGlued, Multicoequalizer.π_desc_assoc, Multicoequalizer.π_desc_assoc]
457	  exact h _
458	
459	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
460	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
461	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
462	theorem ι_glueMorphisms (𝒰 : OpenCover.{v} X) {Y : Scheme} (f : ∀ x, 𝒰.X x ⟶ Y)
463	    (hf : ∀ x y, pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y) ≫ f x = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ f y)
464	    (x : 𝒰.I₀) : 𝒰.f x ≫ 𝒰.glueMorphisms f hf = f x := by
465	  refine Cover.hom_ext (𝒰.ulift.pullback₁ (𝒰.f x)) _ _ fun i ↦ ?_
466	  dsimp only [Precoverage.ZeroHypercover.pullback₁_toPreZeroHypercover,
467	    PreZeroHypercover.pullback₁_X, ulift_X, ulift_f, PreZeroHypercover.pullback₁_f]
468	  simp_rw [pullback.condition_assoc, ← ulift_f, ← ι_fromGlued, Category.assoc, glueMorphisms,
469	    IsIso.hom_inv_id_assoc, ulift_f, hf]
470	  simp [CategoryTheory.GlueData.ι]
471	
472	end Cover
473	
474	lemma hom_ext_of_forall {X Y : Scheme} (f g : X ⟶ Y)
475	    (H : ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ U.ι ≫ f = U.ι ≫ g) : f = g := by
476	  choose U hxU hU using H
477	  let 𝒰 : X.OpenCover := {
478	    I₀ := X, X i := (U i), f i := (U i).ι,
479	    mem₀ := by
480	      rw [presieve₀_mem_precoverage_iff]
481	      refine ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨x, by simpa using hxU x⟩, inferInstance⟩ }
482	  exact 𝒰.hom_ext _ _ hU
483	
484	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
485	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
486	-- TODO: generalize to covers in subcanonical topologies
487	open pullback in
488	attribute [local simp] condition condition_assoc in
489	instance : (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms Scheme).IsLocalAtTarget zariskiPrecoverage :=
490	  .mk_of_isStableUnderBaseChange fun {X Y} f (𝒰 : Y.OpenCover) (H : ∀ i, IsIso _) ↦
491	    ⟨𝒰.glueMorphisms (fun i ↦ inv (snd f (𝒰.f i)) ≫ fst _ _) fun i j ↦ by
492	    rw [← cancel_epi ((pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _).hom ≫ map (fst f (𝒰.f i) ≫ f)
493	      (𝒰.f j) (𝒰.f i) (𝒰.f j) (snd _ _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp) (by simp))]
494	    simp, Cover.hom_ext (𝒰.pullback₁ f) _ _ fun i ↦ by simp, Cover.hom_ext 𝒰 _ _ fun i ↦ by simp⟩

496	/-!
497	
498	## Locally directed gluing
499	
500	We say that a diagram of open immersions is "locally directed" if for any `V, W ⊆ U` in the diagram,
501	`V ∩ W` is a union of elements in the diagram. Equivalently, for every `x ∈ U` in the diagram,
502	the set of elements containing `x` is directed (and hence the name).
503	
504	For such a diagram, we can glue them directly since the gluing conditions are always satisfied.
505	The intended usage is to provide the following instances:
506	- `∀ {i j} (f : i ⟶ j), IsOpenImmersion (F.map f)`
507	- `(F ⋙ forget).IsLocallyDirected`
508	
509	and to directly use the `colimit` API.
510	Also see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsLocallyDirected.openCover` for the open cover of the `colimit`.
511	
512	-/
513	section IsLocallyDirected
514	
515	open TopologicalSpace.Opens
516	
517	universe w
518	
519	variable {J : Type w} [Category.{v} J] (F : J ⥤ Scheme.{u})
520	variable [∀ {i j} (f : i ⟶ j), IsOpenImmersion (F.map f)]
521	
522	namespace IsLocallyDirected
523	
524	/-- (Implementation detail)
525	The intersection `V` in the glue data associated to a locally directed diagram. -/
526	noncomputable
527	def V (i j : J) : (F.obj i).Opens := ⨆ (k : Σ k, (k ⟶ i) × (k ⟶ j)), (F.map k.2.1).opensRange
528	
529	lemma V_self (i) : V F i i = ⊤ :=
530	  top_le_iff.mp (le_iSup_of_le ⟨i, 𝟙 _, 𝟙 _⟩ (by simp [Scheme.Hom.opensRange_of_isIso]))
531	
532	variable [(F ⋙ forget).IsLocallyDirected]
533	
534	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
535	lemma exists_of_pullback_V_V {i j k : J} (x : pullback (C := Scheme) (V F i j).ι (V F i k).ι) :
536	    ∃ (l : J) (fi : l ⟶ i) (fj : l ⟶ j) (fk : l ⟶ k)
537	      (α : F.obj l ⟶ pullback (V F i j).ι (V F i k).ι) (z : F.obj l),
538	      IsOpenImmersion α ∧
539	      α ≫ pullback.fst _ _ = (F.map fi).isoOpensRange.hom ≫
540	        (F.obj i).homOfLE (le_iSup_of_le ⟨l, _, fj⟩ le_rfl) ∧
541	      α ≫ pullback.snd _ _ = (F.map fi).isoOpensRange.hom ≫
542	        (F.obj i).homOfLE (le_iSup_of_le ⟨l, _, fk⟩ le_rfl) ∧
543	      α z = x := by
544	  obtain ⟨k₁, y₁, hy₁⟩ := mem_iSup.mp ((pullback.fst (C := Scheme) _ _) x).2
545	  obtain ⟨k₂, y₂, hy₂⟩ := mem_iSup.mp ((pullback.snd (C := Scheme) _ _) x).2
546	  obtain ⟨l, hli, hlk, z, rfl, rfl⟩ :=
547	    (F ⋙ forget).exists_map_eq_of_isLocallyDirected k₁.2.1 k₂.2.1 y₁ y₂
548	      (by simpa [hy₁, hy₂] using congr($(pullback.condition (f := (V F i j).ι)) x))
549	  let α : F.obj l ⟶ pullback (V F i j).ι (V F i k).ι :=
550	    pullback.lift
551	      ((F.map (hli ≫ k₁.2.1)).isoOpensRange.hom ≫ Scheme.homOfLE _
552	        (le_iSup_of_le ⟨l, hli ≫ k₁.2.1, hli ≫ k₁.2.2⟩ le_rfl))
553	      ((F.map (hli ≫ k₁.2.1)).isoOpensRange.hom ≫ Scheme.homOfLE _
554	        (le_iSup_of_le ⟨l, hli ≫ k₁.2.1, hlk ≫ k₂.2.2⟩ le_rfl))
555	      (by simp)
556	  have : IsOpenImmersion α := by
557	    apply +allowSynthFailures IsOpenImmersion.of_comp
558	    · exact (inferInstance : IsOpenImmersion (pullback.fst (V F i j).ι (V F i k).ι))
559	    · simp only [limit.lift_π, PullbackCone.mk_π_app, α]
560	      infer_instance
561	  have : α z = x := by
562	    apply (pullback.fst (C := Scheme) _ _).isOpenEmbedding.injective
563	    apply (V F i j).ι.isOpenEmbedding.injective
564	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, pullback.lift_fst_assoc]
565	    simpa using hy₁
566	  exact ⟨l, hli ≫ k₁.2.1, hli ≫ k₁.2.2, hlk ≫ k₂.2.2, α, z, ‹_›, by simp [α], by simp [α], ‹_›⟩
567	
568	variable [Quiver.IsThin J]
569	
570	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
571	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
572	lemma fst_inv_eq_snd_inv
573	    {i j : J} (k₁ k₂ : (k : J) × (k ⟶ i) × (k ⟶ j)) {U : (F.obj i).Opens}
574	    (h₁ : (F.map k₁.2.1).opensRange ≤ U) (h₂ : (F.map k₂.2.1).opensRange ≤ U) :
575	    pullback.fst ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₁) ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₂) ≫
576	      (F.map k₁.2.1).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ F.map k₁.2.2 =
577	    pullback.snd ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₁) ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₂) ≫
578	      (F.map k₂.2.1).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ F.map k₂.2.2 := by
579	  apply Scheme.hom_ext_of_forall
580	  intro x
581	  obtain ⟨l, hli, hlj, y, hy₁, hy₂⟩ := (F ⋙ forget).exists_map_eq_of_isLocallyDirected k₁.2.1 k₂.2.1
582	    ((pullback.fst _ _ ≫ (F.map k₁.2.1).isoOpensRange.inv) x)
583	    ((pullback.snd _ _ ≫ (F.map k₂.2.1).isoOpensRange.inv) x) (by
584	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
585	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, Hom.comp_base, TopCat.hom_comp, ContinuousMap.comp_apply,
586	        TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk]
587	      simp only [← Hom.comp_apply]
588	      congr 5
589	      simpa using congr($(pullback.condition (f := (F.obj i).homOfLE h₁)
590	        (g := (F.obj i).homOfLE h₂)) ≫ Scheme.Opens.ι _))
591	  let α : F.obj l ⟶ pullback ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₁) ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₂) :=
592	    pullback.lift
593	      (F.map hli ≫ (F.map k₁.2.1).isoOpensRange.hom)
594	      (F.map hlj ≫ (F.map k₂.2.1).isoOpensRange.hom)
595	      (by simp [← cancel_mono (Scheme.Opens.ι _), ← Functor.map_comp,
596	        Subsingleton.elim (hli ≫ k₁.2.1) (hlj ≫ k₂.2.1)])
597	  have : IsOpenImmersion α := by
598	    have : IsOpenImmersion (α ≫ pullback.fst _ _) := by
599	      simp only [pullback.lift_fst, α]; infer_instance
600	    exact .of_comp _ (pullback.fst _ _)
601	  have : α y = x := by
602	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map, Hom.comp_base,
603	      TopCat.hom_comp, ContinuousMap.comp_apply] at hy₁
604	    apply (pullback.fst ((F.obj i).homOfLE h₁) _).isOpenEmbedding.injective
605	    simp only [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, α, pullback.lift_fst]
606	    simp_all
607	  refine ⟨α.opensRange, ⟨y, this⟩, ?_⟩
608	  rw [← cancel_epi α.isoOpensRange.hom]
609	  simp [α, ← Functor.map_comp, Subsingleton.elim (hli ≫ k₁.2.2) (hlj ≫ k₂.2.2)]
610	
611	/-- (Implementation detail)
612	The inclusion map `V i j ⟶ F j` in the glue data associated to a locally directed diagram. -/
613	def tAux (i j : J) : (V F i j).toScheme ⟶ F.obj j :=
614	  (Scheme.Opens.iSupOpenCover _).glueMorphisms
615	    (fun k ↦ (F.map k.2.1).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ F.map k.2.2) fun k₁ k₂ ↦ by
616	      dsimp [Scheme.Opens.iSupOpenCover]
617	      apply fst_inv_eq_snd_inv F
618	
619	@[reassoc]
620	lemma homOfLE_tAux (i j : J) {k : J} (fi : k ⟶ i) (fj : k ⟶ j) :
621	    (F.obj i).homOfLE (le_iSup_of_le ⟨k, fi, fj⟩ le_rfl) ≫
622	      tAux F i j = (F.map fi).isoOpensRange.inv ≫ F.map fj :=
623	  (Scheme.Opens.iSupOpenCover (J := Σ k, (k ⟶ i) × (k ⟶ j)) _).ι_glueMorphisms _ _ ⟨k, fi, fj⟩
624	
625	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
626	/-- (Implementation detail)
627	The transition map `V i j ⟶ V j i` in the glue data associated to a locally directed diagram. -/

807	    fun i ↦ (glueData F).openCover) J F.obj (colimit.ι F)
808	    ((equivShrink J).trans <| (Equiv.uniqueSigma fun (_ : Unit) ↦ Shrink J).symm)
809	    (fun _ ↦ F.mapIso (eqToIso (by simp [GlueData.openCover, glueData]))) fun i ↦ by
810	  change colimit.ι F i = _ ≫ (glueData F).ι (equivShrink J i) ≫ _

817	lemma ι_eq_ι_iff {i j : J} {xi : F.obj i} {xj : F.obj j} :
818	    colimit.ι F i xi = colimit.ι F j xj ↔
819	      ∃ k fi fj, ∃ (x : F.obj k), F.map fi x = xi ∧ F.map fj x = xj := by
820	  constructor; swap
821	  · rintro ⟨k, fi, fj, x, rfl, rfl⟩; simp only [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, colimit.w]
822	  obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := (equivShrink J).symm.surjective i
823	  obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := (equivShrink J).symm.surjective j
824	  rw [← ((isColimit F).coconePointUniqueUpToIso
825	    (colimit.isColimit F)).inv.isOpenEmbedding.injective.eq_iff]
826	  simp only [Limits.colimit, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
827	    colimit.comp_coconePointUniqueUpToIso_inv, cocone, glueDataι_naturality]
828	  refine ?_ ∘ ((glueData F).ι_eq_iff _ _ _ _).mp
829	  dsimp +instances only [GlueData.Rel]
830	  rintro ⟨x, rfl, rfl⟩
831	  obtain ⟨⟨k, ki, kj⟩, y, hy : F.map ki y = (glueData F).f i j x⟩ := mem_iSup.mp x.2
832	  refine ⟨k, ki, kj, y, hy, ?_⟩
833	  obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := (equivShrink J).symm.surjective k
834	  apply ((glueData F).ι _).isOpenEmbedding.injective
835	  simp only [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Category.assoc, GlueData.glue_condition]
836	  trans (glueData F).ι k y
837	  · simp [← glueDataι_naturality F kj]; rfl
838	  · simp [← glueDataι_naturality F ki, ← hy]; rfl
839

843	  obtain ⟨i, xi, h⟩ :=
844	    (IsLocallyDirected.glueData F).ι_jointly_surjective
845	      (((IsLocallyDirected.isColimit F).coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _)).inv x)

849	  congr 5

851	    (show i = ((equivShrink J) ((equivShrink J).symm i)) by simp)
852	  simp [cocone, eqToHom_map, ← this]

2464	restrictions of `q` are epi. The joint-reflection lemma is the missing ingredient —
2465	it shares its proof skeleton with `isIso_glueRestrictionHom` (mono-ness of
2466	`E ⟶ ∏ (ι_I)_* M_I`). -/
2467	theorem tautologicalQuotient_epi (d r : ℕ) : Epi (tautologicalQuotient d r) := by

2480	          chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2 := by

2485	        (fun J J' K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r J J' K)
2486	        (fun J => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r J)

2488	          (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2)) I

2490	            ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).homEquiv _ _).symm
2491	            (tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
2492	          = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).hom ≫
2493	            chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2 := by
2494	        rw [tautologicalQuotientComponent]
2495	        exact Equiv.symm_apply_apply _ _
2496	      exact h.trans h2

2499	            chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2) ≫ (universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I).inv :=
2500	      (Iso.eq_comp_inv _).mpr hptq
2501	    rw [heq]
2502	    -- `(iso ≫ u^I) ≫ iso` is epi: `u^I` is the split-epi chart quotient, the rest are isos.
2503	    -- Use `epi_comp'` (explicit `Epi` args) to feed `chartQuotientMap_epi` by defeq, avoiding

2522	`ψ : T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` realizes the forward direction of the universal property. -/
2523	noncomputable def tautologicalRankQuotient (d r : ℕ) : RankQuotient r d (scheme d r) where

2532	*chart composite* is `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (the `I`-indexed coordinate inclusion
2533	followed by the quotient map) and the *chart locus* `T_I ⊆ T` is the largest open on

2538	Γ–Spec adjunction. These glue to the inverse `grPointOfRankQuotient` of the universal
2539	property. -/

437	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
438	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
439	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
440	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
441	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
442	
443	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
444	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
445	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
446	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
447	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
448	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
449	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
450	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
451	`chartTransition`. -/
452	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
453	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
454	      = eqToIso (congrArg
455	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
456	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
457	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
458	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
459	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
460	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
461	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
462	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
463	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
464	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
465	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
466	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso

468	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
469	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
470	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
471	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
472	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
473	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
474	      = Iso.refl _ := by
475	    apply Iso.ext
476	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
477	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
478	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
479	  simp only [bundleTransition]

490	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=

493	/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)
494	
495	The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
496	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
497	Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
498	`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
499	are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
500	helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/

502	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.mul_submatrix_col` (private there). -/
503	private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
504	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
505	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
506	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
507	
508	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_nonsing_inv` (private there). -/
509	private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
510	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
511	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
512	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
513	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
514	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul

51	structure GlueData where
52	  /-- The index type `J` of a gluing datum -/
53	  J : Type v
54	  /-- For each `i : J`, an object `U i` -/
55	  U : J → C
56	  /-- For each `i j : J`, an object `V i j` -/
57	  V : J × J → C
58	  /-- For each `i j : J`, a monomorphism `f i j : V i j ⟶ U i` -/
59	  f : ∀ i j, V (i, j) ⟶ U i
60	  f_mono : ∀ i j, Mono (f i j) := by infer_instance
61	  f_hasPullback : ∀ i j k, HasPullback (f i j) (f i k) := by infer_instance
62	  f_id : ∀ i, IsIso (f i i) := by infer_instance
63	  /-- For each `i j : J`, a transition map `t i j : V i j ⟶ V j i` -/
64	  t : ∀ i j, V (i, j) ⟶ V (j, i)
65	  t_id : ∀ i, t i i = 𝟙 _
66	  /-- The morphism via which `V i j ×[U i] V i k ⟶ V i j ⟶ V j i` factors through
67	  `V j k ×[U j] V j i ⟶ V j i` -/
68	  t' : ∀ i j k, pullback (f i j) (f i k) ⟶ pullback (f j k) (f j i)
69	  t_fac : ∀ i j k, t' i j k ≫ pullback.snd _ _ = pullback.fst _ _ ≫ t i j
70	  cocycle : ∀ i j k, t' i j k ≫ t' j k i ≫ t' k i j = 𝟙 _

663	  · simp

665	/-- Two open immersions with equal range are isomorphic. -/
666	def isoOfRangeEq [IsOpenImmersion g] (e : Set.range f = Set.range g) : X ≅ Y where
667	  hom := lift g f (le_of_eq e)
668	  inv := lift f g (le_of_eq e.symm)
669	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← cancel_mono f]; simp
670	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← cancel_mono g]; simp
671	
672	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
673	lemma isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Z) (g : Y ⟶ Z)
674	    [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] (e : Set.range f = Set.range g) :
675	    (isoOfRangeEq f g e).hom ≫ g = f :=
676	  lift_fac _ _ (le_of_eq e)
677	
678	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
679	lemma isoOfRangeEq_inv_fac {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Z) (g : Y ⟶ Z)
680	    [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] (e : Set.range f = Set.range g) :
681	    (isoOfRangeEq f g e).inv ≫ f = g :=
682	  lift_fac _ _ (le_of_eq e.symm)

684	theorem app_eq_invApp_app_of_comp_eq_aux {X Y U : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ U) (g : U ⟶ X) (fg : Y ⟶ X)
685	    (H : fg = f ≫ g) [h : IsOpenImmersion g] (V : U.Opens) :
686	    f ⁻¹ᵁ V = fg ⁻¹ᵁ (g ''ᵁ V) := by
687	  simp_all

690	/-- The `fg` argument is to avoid nasty stuff about dependent types. -/
691	theorem app_eq_appIso_inv_app_of_comp_eq {X Y U : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ U) (g : U ⟶ X) (fg : Y ⟶ X)
692	    (H : fg = f ≫ g) [h : IsOpenImmersion g] (V : U.Opens) :
693	    f.app V = (g.appIso V).inv ≫ fg.app (g ''ᵁ V) ≫ Y.presheaf.map
694	      (eqToHom <| IsOpenImmersion.app_eq_invApp_app_of_comp_eq_aux f g fg H V).op := by
695	  subst H
696	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_app, Category.assoc, Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, f.naturality_assoc,
697	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, Quiver.Hom.unop_op, eqToHom_map, eqToHom_trans,
698	    eqToHom_op, eqToHom_refl, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]

701	    (V : U.Opens) :

703	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom <| app_eq_invApp_app_of_comp_eq_aux _ _ _ (lift_fac ..).symm V).op :=
704	  IsOpenImmersion.app_eq_appIso_inv_app_of_comp_eq _ _ _ (lift_fac _ _ _).symm _
705

708	    (H' : f ⁻¹ᵁ iV.opensRange = iU.opensRange) : IsPullback g iU iV f := by

5590	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):

5592	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.

5595	pair (`tautologicalRankQuotient`); naturality (`homEquiv_comp`) is the already-proven
5596	pseudofunctoriality `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at the tautological point. The
5597	inverse is the chart-by-chart construction `grPointOfRankQuotient`; the two inverse laws
5598	are the remaining content (they consume the chart restriction isomorphisms
5599	`universalQuotient_restrictionIso` and the glued-scheme universal property). -/

45	class JointlySurjective (K : Precoverage Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
46	  exists_eq {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : Presieve X) (hS : S ∈ K X) (x : X) :
47	    ∃ (Y : Scheme.{u}) (g : Y ⟶ X), S g ∧ x ∈ Set.range g

60	  use i, y

63	def Cover.idx [JointlySurjective K] (𝒰 : X.Cover K) (x : X) : 𝒰.I₀ :=
64	  (𝒰.exists_eq x).choose
65	
66	lemma Cover.covers [JointlySurjective K] (𝒰 : X.Cover K) (x : X) :
67	    x ∈ Set.range (𝒰.f (𝒰.idx x)) :=
68	  (𝒰.exists_eq x).choose_spec

72	  rw [Set.eq_univ_iff_forall]
73	  intro x
74	  rw [Set.mem_iUnion]
75	  exact 𝒰.exists_eq x

623	  ext x
624	  by_cases hx : x ∈ Set.range iU
625	  · obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hx

630	    · rintro ⟨y, hy, e : iV y = f x⟩

635	/-- The universal property of open immersions:
636	For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Z`, given any morphism of schemes `g : Y ⟶ Z` whose topological
637	image is contained in the image of `f`, we can lift this morphism to a unique `Y ⟶ X` that

554	  change IsOpenImmersion (_ ≫ f)

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Precoverage
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
10	
11	/-!
12	# 0-hypercovers
13	
14	Given a coverage `J` on a category `C`, we define the type
15	of `0`-hypercovers of an object `S : C`. They consist of a covering family
16	of morphisms `X i ⟶ S` indexed by a type `I₀` such that the induced presieve is in `J`.
17	
18	We define this with respect to a coverage and not to a Grothendieck topology, because this
19	yields more control over the components of the cover.
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	universe w'' w' w v u
25	
26	namespace CategoryTheory
27	
28	open Category Limits
29	
30	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
31	
32	/-- The categorical data that is involved in a `0`-hypercover of an object `S`. This
33	consists of a family of morphisms `f i : X i ⟶ S` for `i : I₀`. -/
34	@[ext]
35	structure PreZeroHypercover (S : C) where
36	  /-- the index type of the covering of `S` -/
37	  I₀ : Type w
38	  /-- the objects in the covering of `S` -/
39	  X (i : I₀) : C
40	  /-- the morphisms in the covering of `S` -/
41	  f (i : I₀) : X i ⟶ S
42	
43	namespace PreZeroHypercover
44	
45	variable {S T : C} (E : PreZeroHypercover.{w} S) (F : PreZeroHypercover.{w'} S)
46	
47	/-- The assumption that the pullback of `X i₁` and `X i₂` over `S` exists
48	for any `i₁` and `i₂`. -/
49	abbrev HasPullbacks := ∀ (i₁ i₂ : E.I₀), HasPullback (E.f i₁) (E.f i₂)
50	
51	/-- The presieve of `S` that is generated by the morphisms `f i : X i ⟶ S`. -/
52	abbrev presieve₀ : Presieve S := .ofArrows _ E.f
53	
54	@[simp]
55	lemma presieve₀_f (i : E.I₀) : E.presieve₀ (E.f i) := ⟨i⟩
56	
57	grind_pattern presieve₀_f => E.presieve₀ (E.f i)
58	
59	/-- The sieve of `S` that is generated by the morphisms `f i : X i ⟶ S`. -/
60	abbrev sieve₀ : Sieve S := .ofArrows _ E.f
61	
62	lemma sieve₀_f (i : E.I₀) : E.sieve₀ (E.f i) := ⟨_, 𝟙 _, E.f i, ⟨i⟩, by simp⟩
63	
64	grind_pattern sieve₀_f => E.sieve₀ (E.f i)
65	
66	/-- The pre-`0`-hypercover defined by a single morphism. -/
67	@[simps]
68	def singleton (f : S ⟶ T) : PreZeroHypercover.{w} T where
69	  I₀ := PUnit
70	  X _ := S
71	  f _ := f
72	
73	@[simp]
74	lemma presieve₀_singleton (f : S ⟶ T) : (singleton f).presieve₀ = .singleton f := by
75	  simp [singleton, presieve₀, Presieve.ofArrows_pUnit]
76	
77	instance (f : S ⟶ T) : Unique (PreZeroHypercover.singleton f).I₀ :=
78	  inferInstanceAs <| Unique PUnit
79	
80	variable (S) in
81	/-- The empty pre-`0`-hypercover. -/
82	@[simps]
83	def empty : PreZeroHypercover.{w} S where
84	  I₀ := PEmpty
85	  X := PEmpty.elim
86	  f i := i.elim
87	
88	instance : IsEmpty (empty S).I₀ := inferInstanceAs <| IsEmpty PEmpty
89	
90	@[simp]

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 David Loeffler. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: David Loeffler
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sets.Opens
9	
10	/-!
11	# Open covers
12	
13	We define `IsOpenCover` as a predicate on indexed families of open sets in a topological space `X`,
14	asserting that their union is `X`. This is an example of a declaration whose name is actually
15	longer than its content; but giving it a name serves as a way of standardizing API.
16	-/
17	
18	@[expose] public section
19	
20	open Set Topology
21	
22	namespace TopologicalSpace
23	
24	/-- An indexed family of open sets whose union is `X`. -/
25	def IsOpenCover {ι X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] (u : ι → Opens X) : Prop :=
26	  iSup u = ⊤
27	
28	variable {ι κ X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] {u : ι → Opens X}
29	  [TopologicalSpace Y] {v : κ → Opens Y}
30	
31	namespace IsOpenCover
32	
33	lemma mk (h : iSup u = ⊤) : IsOpenCover u := h
34	
35	lemma of_sets {v : ι → Set X} (h_open : ∀ i, IsOpen (v i)) (h_iUnion : ⋃ i, v i = univ) :
36	    IsOpenCover (fun i ↦ ⟨v i, h_open i⟩) := by
37	  simp [IsOpenCover, h_iUnion]
38	
39	lemma iSup_eq_top (hu : IsOpenCover u) : ⨆ i, u i = ⊤ := hu
40	
41	lemma iSup_set_eq_univ (hu : IsOpenCover u) : ⋃ i, (u i : Set X) = univ := by
42	  simpa [← SetLike.coe_set_eq] using hu.iSup_eq_top
43	
44	/-- Pullback of a covering of `Y` by a continuous map `X → Y`, giving a covering of `X` with the
45	same index type. -/
46	lemma comap (hv : IsOpenCover v) (f : C(X, Y)) : IsOpenCover fun k ↦ (v k).comap f := by
47	  simp [IsOpenCover, ← preimage_iUnion, hv.iSup_set_eq_univ]
48	
49	lemma exists_mem (hu : IsOpenCover u) (a : X) : ∃ i, a ∈ u i := by
50	  simpa [← hu.iSup_set_eq_univ] using mem_univ a
51	
52	lemma exists_mem_nhds (hu : IsOpenCover u) (a : X) : ∃ i, (u i : Set X) ∈ 𝓝 a :=
53	  match hu.exists_mem a with | ⟨i, hi⟩ => ⟨i, (u i).isOpen.mem_nhds hi⟩
54	
55	lemma iUnion_inter (hu : IsOpenCover u) (s : Set X) :
56	    ⋃ i, s ∩ u i = s := by
57	  simp [← inter_iUnion, hu.iSup_set_eq_univ]
58	
59	lemma isTopologicalBasis (hu : IsOpenCover u)
60	    {B : ∀ i, Set (Set (u i))} (hB : ∀ i, IsTopologicalBasis (B i)) :
61	    IsTopologicalBasis (⋃ i, (Subtype.val '' ·) '' B i) :=
62	  isTopologicalBasis_of_cover (fun i ↦ (u i).2) hu.iSup_set_eq_univ hB
63	
64	end IsOpenCover
65	
66	lemma Opens.IsBasis.isOpenCover {S : Set (Opens X)} (hS : Opens.IsBasis S) :
67	    IsOpenCover (fun U : S ↦ (U : Opens X)) :=
68	  top_le_iff.mp (subset_trans hS.2.superset (by simp))

592	namespace Scheme.Modules
593	
594	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
595	
596	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
597	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
598	
599	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
600	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
601	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
602	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
603	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
604	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
605	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
606	
607	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
608	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
609	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
610	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
611	
612	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
613	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
614	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
615	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
616	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
617	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
618	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
619

1221	/-! ### Epimorphisms between free sheaves split over affines
1222	
1223	The Nakayama/covering step of `chartLocus_isOpenCover` needs pointwise (fibre-level)
1224	surjectivity of an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Over an affine base this needs no
1225	stalk theory at all: the tilde functor `ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules` is fully
1226	faithful and additive, identifies the free sheaf with the tilde of the free module
1227	(`tildeFinsupp`), epimorphisms of modules are surjections, and free modules are
1228	projective — so any epimorphism between free sheaves on `Spec R` is *split*, and its
1229	presenting matrix admits a right inverse over the global sections. -/
1230	
1231	/-- **Epimorphisms between free sheaves of modules on `Spec R` split**: the splitting is
1232	transported through the fully faithful `tilde.functor` from the projectivity of the free
1233	module `Fin d →₀ R`. Project-local. -/
1234	lemma exists_section_of_epi_free_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1235	    (ψ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
1236	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) [Epi ψ] :
1237	    ∃ Φ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
1238	        SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r),
1239	      Φ ≫ ψ = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
1240	  -- the tilde-conjugate of `ψ` and its module-level preimage under full faithfulness
1241	  let ψt : tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R)) ⟶ tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1242	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv
1243	  let g : ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R) :=
1244	    tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimage ψt
1245	  have hg : tilde.map g = ψt := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.map_preimage ψt
1246	  haveI hiso_r : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin r)).hom := inferInstance
1247	  haveI hiso_d : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin d)).inv := inferInstance
1248	  haveI hψt : Epi ψt := by
1249	    -- fully explicit: the `(Spec R).Modules` / `(Spec (.of ↥R)).Modules` defeq blocks
1250	    -- automatic instance search across the composite
1251	    haveI h1 : Epi (ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv) :=
1252	      @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ ψ ‹Epi ψ› _ (IsIso.epi_of_iso _)
1253	    exact @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom (IsIso.epi_of_iso _) _ h1
1254	  haveI hge : Epi g := by
1255	    refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
1256	    show Epi (tilde.map g)
1257	    rw [hg]
1258	    exact hψt
1259	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective g.hom := (ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective g).mp hge
1260	  -- a module-level section of `g` by projectivity of the free module
1261	  obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property g.hom LinearMap.id hsurj
1262	  refine ⟨(tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1263	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom, ?_⟩
1264	  have hcomp : ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g = 𝟙 (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1265	    ModuleCat.hom_ext (by
1266	      rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_id, ModuleCat.hom_ofHom]
1267	      exact hw)
1268	  have hstep : (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ = tilde.map g ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by
1269	    rw [hg]
1270	    simp only [ψt, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
1271	  calc ((tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1272	        (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom) ≫ ψ
1273	      = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1274	        ((tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ) := by simp only [Category.assoc]
1275	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1276	        tilde.map g ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by rw [hstep]
1277	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g) ≫
1278	        (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by rw [← tilde.map_comp_assoc]
1279	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by
1280	        rw [hcomp, tilde.map_id]
1281	    _ = 𝟙 _ := by rw [Category.id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id]
1282	
1283	/-- **Presenting matrices of epimorphisms admit right inverses over `Spec R`**: combine
1284	the splitting `exists_section_of_epi_free_spec` with the matrix presentation of the
1285	section and the rectangular composition law. Project-local. -/
1286	lemma exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1287	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(Spec R, ⊤)) (h : Epi (matrixEndRect M)) :
1288	    ∃ G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) Γ(Spec R, ⊤), M * G = 1 := by
1289	  haveI := h
1290	  obtain ⟨Φ, hΦ⟩ := exists_section_of_epi_free_spec (matrixEndRect M)
1291	  refine ⟨Matrix.of (fun q p => unitEndSection
1292	    (SheafOfModules.ιFree p ≫ Φ ≫ projFree q)), ?_⟩
1293	  have hpres : matrixEndRect (Matrix.of (fun q p => unitEndSection
1294	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree p ≫ Φ ≫ projFree q))) = Φ := matrixEndRect_unitEndSection Φ
1295	  apply matrixEndRect_injective
1296	  rw [← matrixEndRect_comp_rect, hpres, hΦ, matrixEndRect_one]
1297	
1298	/-- **Presenting matrices of epimorphisms admit right inverses over any affine scheme** —
1299	the `Spec` case transported along `S.isoSpec` through the free-pullback comparisons
1300	(`pullback_conj_matrixEndRect`). Project-local; the algebraic heart of the Nakayama
1301	covering step. -/
1302	lemma exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsAffine S] {d r : ℕ}
1303	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (h : Epi (matrixEndRect M)) :
1304	    ∃ G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤), M * G = 1 := by
1305	  -- transport to `Spec Γ(S, ⊤)` along the inverse of `isoSpec`
1306	  haveI h1 : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M)) :=
1307	    @CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv)
1308	      inferInstance _ _ _ h
1309	  haveI h2 : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin r)).inv ≫
1310	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M) ≫
1311	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin d)).hom) := by
1312	    haveI : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M) ≫
1313	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin d)).hom) := epi_comp _ _
1314	    exact epi_comp _ _
1315	  have h3 : Epi (matrixEndRect (M.map
1316	      ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv)))) := by
1317	    rw [← pullback_conj_matrixEndRect S.isoSpec.inv M]
1318	    exact h2
1319	  obtain ⟨G', hG'⟩ := exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec
1320	    (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))) h3
1321	  -- pull the right inverse back along the (iso) global-sections comorphism
1322	  have hcancel : Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom
1323	      = 𝟙 (Γ(S, ⊤)) := by
1324	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id]
1325	    simp
1326	  refine ⟨G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)), ?_⟩
1327	  have hM : (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))).map
1328	      ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) = M := by
1329	    rw [Matrix.map_map]
1330	    refine Matrix.ext (fun p j => ?_)

2253	unchanged. -/
2254	
2255	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
2256	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
2257	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
2258	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
2259	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
2260	  F : T.Modules
2261	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
2262	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
2263	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
2264	  epi : Epi q
2265	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
2266	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
2267	
2268	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
2269	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
2270	coincide). -/
2271	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
2272	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
2273	
2274	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
2275	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
2276	
2277	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
2278	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
2279	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
2280	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
2281	
2282	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
2283	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
2284	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
2285	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
2286	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
2287	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
2288	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
2289	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
2290	
2291	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
2292	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
2293	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
2294	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
2295	
2296	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
2297	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
2298	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
2299	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
2300	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
2301	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
2302	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
2303	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
2304	  epi :=
2305	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
2306	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
2307	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
2308	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
2309	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
2310	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
2311	        x.q x.epi)
2312	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree
2313	
2314	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/

-/

@[expose] public section


noncomputable section

open CategoryTheory.Limits

namespace CategoryTheory

universe v u₁ u₂

variable (C : Type u₁) [Category.{v} C] {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C']

/-- A gluing datum consists of
1. An index type `J`
2. An object `U i` for each `i : J`.
3. An object `V i j` for each `i j : J`.
4. A monomorphism `f i j : V i j ⟶ U i` for each `i j : J`.
5. A transition map `t i j : V i j ⟶ V j i` for each `i j : J`.

such that
6. `f i i` is an isomorphism.
7. `t i i` is the identity.
8. The pullback for `f i j` and `f i k` exists.
9. `V i j ×[U i] V i k ⟶ V i j ⟶ V j i` factors through `V j k ×[U j] V j i ⟶ V j i` via some
    `t' : V i j ×[U i] V i k ⟶ V j k ×[U j] V j i`.
10. `t' i j k ≫ t' j k i ≫ t' k i j = 𝟙 _`.
-/
structure GlueData where
  /-- The index type `J` of a gluing datum -/

1	import Mathlib
2	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
3	
4	/-!
5	# Glue-datum descent for sheaves of modules over a scheme
6	
7	Generic `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` glue-datum / descent layer:
8	`glue` (equalizer-of-pushforwards construction), `glueLift`, and the full
9	restriction isomorphism tower that makes the descent effective.
10	
11	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GlueDescent.tex`.
12	-/
13	
14	universe u
15	
16	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
17	
18	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
19	
20	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
21	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
22	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction.
23	
24	Construction (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): the glued sheaf is built directly as a
25	categorical limit — an **equalizer of pushforwards** — rather than a hand-built presheaf of
26	compatible families. Concretely, `glue` forms the two parallel maps
27	`∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{i,j} (ιᵢ ≫ f_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` (one leg the adjunction unit composed
28	with the pushforward-composition comparison, the other transported across the inverse
29	transition `(g_ij)⁻¹`), and takes their equalizer inside `Scheme.Modules D.glued`. The
30	self-identity (C1) and triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) hypotheses `_hC1`/`_hC2` on the
31	family `g` are NOT consumed in forming the equalizer object (the limit exists for any family
32	of transition maps); they are the descent conditions pinned down downstream when the
33	restriction isomorphisms are produced. The body below and the `_hC1`/`_hC2` signature are

548	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme, so it is built here. -/
549	
550	namespace SheafOfModules
551	
552	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
553	
554	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
555	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
556	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
557	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
558	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
559	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
560	
561	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
562	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
563	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
564	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
565	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
566	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
567	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
568	
569	end SheafOfModules

132	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
133	/-- **Identity coherence, component form.**  For a scheme endomorphism `f'`
134	equal to the identity and a morphism `g` with `f' ≫ g = g`, the composite of
135	the pseudofunctor comparisons collapsing `f'^* g^* V` back to `g^* V` is the
136	identity.  This is `pseudofunctor_right_unitality` conjugated by
137	`pullbackCongr`; it discharges the `map_id` law of both the Quot and the
138	Grassmannian functor. -/
139	lemma pullback_id_app_coherence {Y W : Scheme.{u}} {f' : Y ⟶ Y} (hf : f' = 𝟙 Y)
140	    {g : Y ⟶ W} (hgc : f' ≫ g = g) (V : W.Modules) :
141	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hgc).inv.app V ≫
142	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g).inv.app V ≫
143	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hf).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
144	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) =
145	    𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) := by
146	  subst hf
147	  have law := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality (f := g)
148	  have lawV := congrArg (fun η => η.app V) law
149	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app,
150	    eqToHom_app] at lawV
151	  rw [pullbackCongr_inv_app hgc, pullbackCongr_hom_app rfl]
152	  simp only [eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp]
153	  simp only [eqToHom_refl] at lawV
154	  exact lawV

156	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
157	/-- **Composition coherence, component form.**  For composable scheme
158	morphisms `a`, `b`, a morphism `c` equal to `a ≫ b`, and a reference morphism
159	`g`, the two ways of collapsing `a^* b^* g^* V` to `(c ≫ g)^* V` through the
160	pseudofunctor comparisons agree.  This is `pseudofunctor_associativity`
161	conjugated by `pullbackCongr`; it discharges the `map_comp` law of both the
162	Quot and the Grassmannian functor. -/
163	lemma pullback_comp_app_coherence {W Xs Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z ⟶ Y) (b : Y ⟶ Xs)
164	    {c : Z ⟶ Xs} (hc : c = a ≫ b) (g : Xs ⟶ W) {gb : Y ⟶ W} {gc : Z ⟶ W}
165	    (h₁ : b ≫ g = gb) (h₂ : c ≫ g = gc) (h₃ : a ≫ gb = gc) (V : W.Modules) :
166	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
167	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hc).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
168	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c g).hom.app V ≫
169	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₂).hom.app V =
170	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map
171	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).hom.app V ≫
172	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₁).hom.app V) ≫
173	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a gb).hom.app V ≫
174	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₃).hom.app V := by
175	  subst hc
176	  subst h₁
177	  subst h₃
178	  -- the associativity law, in component form at `V`
179	  have law := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := a) (g := b) (h := g)
180	  have lawV := congrArg (fun η => η.app V) law
181	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
182	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app] at lawV
183	  simp only [Category.id_comp] at lawV
184	  -- `lawV : (pullbackComp a (b ≫ g)).inv.app V ≫
185	  --   (pullback a).map ((pullbackComp b g).inv.app V) ≫
186	  --   (pullbackComp a b).hom.app (g^* V) ≫ (pullbackComp (a ≫ b) g).hom.app V
187	  --   = eqToHom _`
188	  -- move the two invertible prefixes of `lawV` to the right-hand side
189	  have h1 := congrArg
190	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a (b ≫ g)).hom.app V ≫ m) lawV
191	  simp only [Iso.hom_inv_id_app_assoc] at h1
192	  have h2 := congrArg
193	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map
194	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).hom.app V) ≫ m) h1
195	  simp only [← Functor.map_comp_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app,
196	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.id_comp] at h2

199	  --     (pullbackComp a (b ≫ g)).hom.app V ≫ eqToHom _`

243	/-! ## §1. The induced morphism on relative products
244	
245	For the Quot functor over `T : Over S`, the family lives on the relative
246	product `X_T := pullback π T.hom`; a morphism `ψ : T' ⟶ T` of `Over S`
247	induces `Scheme.quotBaseMap π ψ : X_{T'} ⟶ X_T` (the map `id_X ×_S ψ`),
248	functorially, compatibly with both projections, and cartesian over
249	`ψ.left : T' ⟶ T` (`Scheme.quotBaseSquare`). -/
250	
251	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
252	
253	/-- The morphism `X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T` induced by `ψ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over S`
254	(the map `id_X ×_S ψ.left`). -/
255	noncomputable def quotBaseMap (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
256	    (Limits.pullback π T'.hom : Scheme.{u}) ⟶ Limits.pullback π T.hom :=
257	  Limits.pullback.map π T'.hom π T.hom (𝟙 X) ψ.left (𝟙 S) (by simp)
258	    (by simpa using (Over.w ψ).symm)
259	
260	@[reassoc]
261	lemma quotBaseMap_fst (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
262	    quotBaseMap π ψ ≫ pullback.fst π T.hom = pullback.fst π T'.hom := by
263	  simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_fst]
264	
265	@[reassoc]
266	lemma quotBaseMap_snd (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
267	    quotBaseMap π ψ ≫ pullback.snd π T.hom = pullback.snd π T'.hom ≫ ψ.left := by
268	  simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_snd]
269	
270	lemma quotBaseMap_id (π : X ⟶ S) (T : Over S) :
271	    quotBaseMap π (𝟙 T) = 𝟙 (Limits.pullback π T.hom) := by
272	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;>
273	    simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_fst, pullback.lift_snd]
274	
275	lemma quotBaseMap_comp (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' T'' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) (φ : T'' ⟶ T') :
276	    quotBaseMap π (φ ≫ ψ) = quotBaseMap π φ ≫ quotBaseMap π ψ := by
277	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;>
278	    simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_fst, pullback.lift_snd,
279	      pullback.lift_fst_assoc, pullback.lift_snd_assoc]
280	
281	/-- The square
282	```
283	X ×_S T' ──quotBaseMap──→ X ×_S T
284	   │                         │
285	  snd                       snd
286	   ↓                         ↓
287	   T' ────────ψ.left───────→ T
288	```
289	is cartesian: `X ×_S T' = (X ×_S T) ×_T T'`.  This is the square along which
290	flatness and proper support of a Quot family base-change. -/
291	lemma quotBaseSquare (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
292	    IsPullback (quotBaseMap π ψ) (pullback.snd π T'.hom)
293	      (pullback.snd π T.hom) ψ.left := by
294	  have s : IsPullback (quotBaseMap π ψ ≫ pullback.fst π T.hom)
295	      (pullback.snd π T'.hom) π (ψ.left ≫ T.hom) := by
296	    rw [quotBaseMap_fst, Over.w]
